clean_body: | | | | Dear Friend, What happens when politicians are forced to answer real questions, take real votes, and defend their records?This week, we got the answer.In state after state, Freedom Caucus members forced clarity and accountability from the political class. They exposed bad votes, challenged weak leadership, and proved that conservatives can be both right and effective.Here’s what happened. | OKLAHOMA This week, the Oklahoma Freedom Caucus hosted a gubernatorial debate for Republican candidates, and it made one thing clear.If you want to lead Oklahoma, you cannot ignore the Freedom Caucus. Candidates for the state’s top office showed up because they recognize the influence and credibility the Oklahoma Freedom Caucus has built with voters across the state.The debate was moderated by the Network’s own Oklahoma State Director, Nathan Dahm , and it looked very different from a typical political forum. This was not a media-run debate designed to protect candidates. It was a debate built around accountability.Dahm asked questions that forced candidates to take a stand. Here's one example:“Name at least 3 examples of bills passed by the Republican legislature that you would veto as governor for violating your principles.”That question cuts to the core.It forced candidates to choose between blind loyalty to the political establishment or standing on principle and acknowledging when Republicans get it wrong.That is exactly what a Freedom Caucus does.It forces politicians to tell voters the truth.And one candidate chose not to participate. Attorney General Gentner Drummond declined to attend , claiming concerns about fairness.So the Oklahoma Freedom Caucus gave him representation on stage anyway.An empty suit stood behind a podium in his place.It was a fitting visual.The Oklahoma Freedom Caucus is not just participating in state politics. It is shaping it. | WYOMINGWyoming Freedom Caucus member Rep. John Bear laid out a simple framework this week for how to tell if conservatives are actually being effective. years, the GOP Swamp has claimed that Republicans can be principled or effective, but not both.Bear says that is simply not true. In fact, he argues that backlash is often the clearest sign that you are doing exactly what you were elected to do.He lays out three signs that you are being effective:Big government Republicans attack youThe media starts lying about youThe deep state pulls out all the stops to stop youIn other words, if the establishment is upset, you are probably getting results.As Bear put it:“You know you are effective AND right if you represent the people, you do what you said you would do when you campaigned, and the weaksauce Republicans start screeching.”That is the Freedom Caucus model in plain English. | RADIO CLIP OF THE WEEK From our weekly radio show (), South Dakota Freedom Caucus Vice Chair Sen. John Carley explains how South Dakota passed legislation to ensure that only American citizens can vote in their state elections.It is a simple principle. Only American citizens should vote in American elections.Some Republicans actually joined the Democrats to oppose it, but the SDFC forced everyone to vote and they passed itMeanwhile, Congress continues to stall on the SAVE America Act. South Dakota is represented by Senate Majority Leader John Thune , who has the authority to move the bill forward but is dragging his feet.The contrast is clear.State Freedom Caucuses are delivering results while Washington makes excuses not to fight. Subscribe on (or on your preferred podcast platform)Follow us on X \- us on TikTok \- us on Facebook \- | QUICK HITS ⚡️Pennsylvania: House Democrats introduced a noncontroversial resolution honoring Women’s History Month. But Pennsylvania Freedom Caucus member Rep. Aaron Bernstine wanted the Left to clarify something so he offered an amendment that correctly defines what a woman is. Rather than vote on it, Democrats shelved their own resolution! Take the time and . It's worth it. ⚡️Maryland: After fighting it tooth and nail, every Maryland Freedom Caucus member naturally voted against the largest budget in state history, but then they took the opportunity to go one step further. They Republican leadership for supporting it. Voting is one thing. Accountability is another. The people deserve to know. ⚡️Montana: TheMontana Freedom Caucus "Republican" State Rep. Llew Jones for hiring a Democrat Party operative to run his campaign. For anyone paying attention, this isn't a surprise since Jones consistently sides with the Democrats to raise taxes and spending. As the Montana Freedom Caucus puts it, just be honest and run as a Democrat! | HELP US KEEP BUILDING The victories you read about in this email do not happen on their own.They happen because Freedom Caucus members are willing to challenge the political establishment, force tough votes, and tell voters the truth about what is really happening in their state capitols. The State Freedom Caucus Network exists to support these lawmakers, expand the Freedom Caucus model into more states, and help conservatives fight back where it matters most.If you want to help us keep building this movement, please support our work today. We'll be back with another update soon. Until then, please be sure to our efforts so that we can support our State Freedom Caucuses!In Liberty, Andy RothPresident State Freedom Caucus Network | |

name: Deb Haaland
subject: ribbon skirt + mocs [photos inside]
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO**
body:
| | | [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/134?t=1&akid=8674%2E400544%2EmWjUOp)
---
[](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/7253?refcode=jtk514-fr-nd&t=2&akid=8674%2E400544%2EmWjUOp)
Hi friend, it's Deb Haaland. No matter where I go, I'm a proud Pueblo woman.

When I wear my mocs or my ribbon skirt, I'm reminded of how powerful traditional clothing is in sharing our heritage. From my ancestral homelands to the halls of Congress, I rock my mocs everywhere I go. And it reminds me to honor the shoulders I stand on.

In a minute, I'm going to share some of the other places I've **rocked my ribbon skirt and mocs.** [ _**But before I do, I have to ask:**_**_Will you please help us build on this winning momentum by giving $5 to my campaign right now?_**](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/7647?refcode=jtk514-fr-nd&t=3&akid=8674%2E400544%2EmWjUOp)
| | [Donate](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/7253?refcode=jtk514-fr-nd&t=4&akid=8674%2E400544%2EmWjUOp)
---
| | On the day I was sworn in as Secretary of the Interior, I decided to wear my white moccasins and a handmade ribbon skirt adorned with butterflies, stars, and corn. No matter where I go, I am a proud Pueblo woman first — including at the Department of the Interior.
---
[](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/7253?refcode=jtk514-fr-nd&t=5&akid=8674%2E400544%2EmWjUOp)
---
| | On the first day of the White House Tribal Nations Summit in 2021, I wore my beaded moccasins in solidarity with our Indigenous people across the country. I am incredibly grateful that together, our administration made extraordinary accomplishments for Indian Country — enduring progress that would make our ancestors proud.
---
[](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/7253?refcode=jtk514-fr-nd&t=6&akid=8674%2E400544%2EmWjUOp)
---
| | From my ancestral homelands here in New Mexico to the halls of Congress — and as I filed my candidate petition to add my name to New Mexico's ballot for governor — I rock my ribbon skirt to honor my ancestors.
---
[](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/7253?refcode=jtk514-fr-nd&t=7&akid=8674%2E400544%2EmWjUOp)
---
Friend, history has shown us there’s nothing we can’t accomplish when we work together, which is why I’m counting on the support of people like you as we fight for a New Mexico that works for everyone.
This race is heating up, friend, and with our FIRST public deadline of 2026 and before the primary, I need your help. **I am ready to fight for an education that gives every child the chance to dream big and reach their full potential here in New Mexico. Give families a raise by increasing the minimum wage and the child tax credit. Protect affordable healthcare and expand access to treatment and recovery programs for those suffering from addiction.
**
**_[Please, friend: Will you chip in $5 right now so we can build a strong campaign capable of winning this race and making history again?](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/7647?refcode=jtk514-fr-nd&t=8&akid=8674%2E400544%2EmWjUOp)_**
| | [Donate](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/7253?refcode=jtk514-fr-nd&t=9&akid=8674%2E400544%2EmWjUOp)
---
I am so grateful for your support. Thank you for being by my side.

Be fierce,
Deb
|
* * *
| | [Donate >>](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/1101?refcode=jtk514-fr-nd&t=10&akid=8674%2E400544%2EmWjUOp)
---
| | [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/1102?t=11&akid=8674%2E400544%2EmWjUOp)
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
[**Receive Fewer Emails**](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails-dh/?&t=12&akid=8674%2E400544%2EmWjUOp)
[**Update Your Information**](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/update-your-information-dh/?&t=13&akid=8674%2E400544%2EmWjUOp)
[**Unsubscribe**](https://act.debhaaland.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe-dh/?&t=14&akid=8674%2E400544%2EmWjUOp)
Email us: info@debhaaland.com
**PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO**

name: Membership Reminder via DNC
subject: We’re scaling up, can you join us?
body:

Derek, **THERE’S STILL TIME TO CLAIM YOUR OFFICIAL 2026 DNC MEMBER CARD** [](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-april-2026-membercard?refcode=em_20260403_6602_fr&amount=7&refcode2=6602_22930333_He6mZ8&akid=6602%2E22930333%2EHe6mZ8) We’ve officially passed our first end-of-quarter deadline of the election year, and races across the country are heating up fast. Here at the DNC, we’re scaling up our efforts to organize, register new Democratic voters, and elect Democrats all over the country at every level of government. **But we need a surge of grassroots momentum to power this work. We’re asking 10,000 Democrats from across the country to activate their 2026 membership by midnight _tonight_.** Here’s what we have on record for you: **_2026 DNC Member Card Record_** **NAME:** Derek

subject: President Obama is backing our campaign

body:
# [](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/18?refcode=em20260403-1334&t=1&akid=1334%2E294397%2EC7dWNt)
Folks, we have exciting news to share:
**President Obama has endorsed Judge Chris Taylor!**
[](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/31?c0=em_ms_jct_20260403_1_ak.scRYC2&amount=10&refcode=em20260403-1334&t=3&akid=1334%2E294397%2EC7dWNt)
He’s right. Judge Chris Taylor is the only candidate in this race with a proven record of fairness.
That’s why we’re working around the clock to elect her to the Wisconsin Supreme Court. **But with Election Day coming up this Tuesday, we’re running out of time to reach the 53% of voters who are still undecided.**
[**Can you chip in $10 today to help us reach Wisconsinites before they head to the polls?**](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/31?c0=em_ms_jct_20260403_1_ak.scRYC2&amount=10&refcode=em20260403-1334&t=4&akid=1334%2E294397%2EC7dWNt)

_“Ms. Berg, a Minnesota state representative who works as a flight attendant, is trying to jet her way from the galley to the gilded halls of the Capitol.

She is part of a new crop of working-class candidates with atypical backgrounds who Democrats hope can help the party win back blue-collar voters, a key constituency that has been slipping away from them for more than a decade.” _ _“If you look at Congress, there are people there that are independently wealthy,” said Ms. Berg, 52, who earns about $45 an hour as a flight attendant. “Some of them are multimillionaires. They’ve been there forever. They haven’t had to worry about a paycheck in decades. But they’re making decisions about people and their lives without a clear understanding of it.”_ [**You can be a part of Kaela’s historic campaign by rushing a split donation of $20.26 or more today →**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128684710/612054036/578229160?refcode=EM_F_260403Berg_N_IPB_NO_10_G_N_L1&amount=20.26&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQwMDA3YzM3LTYxMmYtZjExMS05YTQ4LTAwMGQzYTE0YjY0MCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=_D5evX72KOelwaQiktESyzzelMvqvxGPAFFhKJByvLk=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately and automatically split between Her Bold Move and Kaela Berg:_ | | [**$20.26 »**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128684712/612054038/578229160?refcode=EM_F_260403Berg_N_IPB_NO_10_G_N_B20.26&express_lane=true&amount=20.26&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQwMDA3YzM3LTYxMmYtZjExMS05YTQ4LTAwMGQzYTE0YjY0MCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=_D5evX72KOelwaQiktESyzzelMvqvxGPAFFhKJByvLk=)

As a state legislator, Kaela has delivered tangible, meaningful progress: defending reproductive freedom, expanding workers’ rights, strengthening labor protections, securing historic investments in public education, lowering costs for families, and helping pass some of the strongest worker and consumer protections in the country. And she is the only candidate in this race with the record and lived experiences necessary to continue delivering results for hardworking families.

As Kaela told us, **“This race isn’t just about a seat — it’s about direction.”** It’s about whether Minnesota will continue to accept the status quo or fight for a future where everyone can thrive. _“In the face of rising extremism and authoritarian threats – we don’t need more of the same. We need leaders who are ready to stand up, speak boldly, and fight for the future Minnesotans deserve. This campaign is about doing exactly that.”_

[**Kaela believes her experience living paycheck to paycheck — like many voters in her district — would make her the strongest candidate for the party in a general election. We are so proud to be supporting this incredible and exceptional candidate. As this NYT article underscores, Kaela needs all the fundraising support she can get. Support Kaela’s campaign and our programs for her by pitching in a split $20.26 or more contribution today.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128684718/612054046/578229160?refcode=EM_F_260403Berg_N_IPB_NO_10_G_N_L2&amount=20.26&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQwMDA3YzM3LTYxMmYtZjExMS05YTQ4LTAwMGQzYTE0YjY0MCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=_D5evX72KOelwaQiktESyzzelMvqvxGPAFFhKJByvLk=)

Thank you,

Ileana

Ileana Paules-Bronet
Head of Messaging

name: Virginians For Fair Elections
subject: Tim Kaine just voted. Now it’s our turn.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Virginians for Fair Elections
body:
# [](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/629?refcode=em20260403-442&t=1&akid=442%2E12592%2EMi4R1y)
Earlier this week in Richmond, Senator Tim Kaine cast his early vote. And he had a clear message for Virginians: **Vote YES on the redistricting amendment.**
With early voting underway and April 21 fast approaching, the moment to leave it all on the table is here right now. What we do will determine whether Virginia voters have the final say in 2026 or whether Republicans will successfully tilt the rules before ballots are even cast.
Senator Kaine knows what’s at stake. We cannot let Trump’s power grabs succeed. We cannot let Republicans stack the deck before the 2026 midterms.
_That’s why he showed up early, and it’s why we need to meet this moment too._
While the momentum behind the YES campaign is building, so is the opposition. There are powerful forces working to confuse voters and protect a system that puts Trump’s whims above Virginians’ voting rights.
**We can’t let that happen.**
Our team is on the ground across Virginia right now reaching voters, sharing the facts, and building the support we need to win. But that work takes resources, especially in these final weeks.
[**If we’re going to level the playing field, we need all hands on deck. Will you chip in $15, $30, or whatever you can today to help us turn out YES votes before April 21?**](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/41?c0=em_ms_vafe_20260403_1.lU-k7H&amount=15&refcode=20260403-442&t=2&akid=442%2E12592%2EMi4R1y)
**[Donate $3 ››](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/41?c0=em_ms_vafe_20260403_1.lU-k7H&amount=3&refcode=20260403-442&t=21&akid=442%2E12592%2EMi4R1y)**
Early voting has already started. Ballots are being cast. And the conversations we have today will shape the outcome of this election.
Senator Kaine did his part this week, now we’re asking you to do yours:
[**Chip in to help us reach more voters, cut through the noise, and get this across the finish line!**](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/41?c0=em_ms_vafe_20260403_1.lU-k7H&amount=3&refcode=20260403-442&t=22&akid=442%2E12592%2EMi4R1y)
Thank you for being part of this,
Virginians for Fair Elections

[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_vafe_2026_footer?refcode=em&t=23&refcode2=442_12592_Mi4R1y&akid=442%2E12592%2EMi4R1y)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://vafe.actionkit.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=24&akid=442%2E12592%2EMi4R1y)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://vafe.actionkit.com/signup/update-contact-information/?&t=25&akid=442%2E12592%2EMi4R1y)
* [Unsubscribe](https://vafe.actionkit.com/signup/unsubscribe/?t=26&akid=442%2E12592%2EMi4R1y)

Contributions or gifts to Virginians for Fair Elections are not tax deductible.

Our team is on the ground across Virginia right now reaching voters, sharing the facts, and building the support we need to win. But that work takes resources, especially in these final weeks. Early voting has already started. Ballots are being cast. And the conversations we have today will shape the outcome of this election. Senator Kaine did his part this week, now we’re asking you to do yours: Thank you for being part of this, Virginians for Fair Elections

Here’s how we got here:
**First:** Republican megadonors – including a Texas oil billionaire – poured millions into this race to back the Trump-endorsed Mike Rogers.

**Then:** Polling showed Haley in a statistical tie with Rogers.

**Now:** The GOP is saying Michigan is their best shot to save their Senate majority – and they’re acting as if they’ve already won.
null, here’s what they’re counting on: that Haley’s grassroots supporters won’t show up.

| Republicans and corporate-beholden leaders want you to think that taking care of one another is the most radical thing this country can do.

name: NRSC HQ
subject: MASSIVE NEWS – PLEASE READ IMMEDIATELY
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
################################# | Peter,

Our party’s position is getting stronger and stronger ahead of the midterms.

Polling shows Republicans have an **8-POINT LEAD** over radical far-Left Democrats.

On top of that, Senate Republicans **OUTRAISED** Senate Democrats by $8 MILLION in 2025!

Given the strong financial position our party is in, we’re not going to ask you for money.

** Instead, we’re laser-focused on securing our lead by focusing our time and resources on the ONE THING that actually wins elections: _[Votes!](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=4aae8b8a28cf49d98e5c0ded6de1d010&_e=9trxGjWzwD0XECy22DL9bdb08h0guJABJ1xh1-CuoSzjIOYKe5zcfSaukTMPmmSXgj_JUP2wNxoZ_msrFtrnbjDiEOoU809oFI8dD6Lolf_mEhNYcS4977PDppjZ3Qi3oY3tLU9Db5_Eug8scs_MzY8lRWJskpoof3fqsUMcFlexmU4iLj2NbMkaINww1KR9Lily9sa0zi-xl26JoTW7LdlDxUjPjUZjDn_1eiMFqefQ782XQD5rOq0gsEbuQTrArbnjNcmPWWz4E9w0y3kK3yWNJvhWdB_5hY4ZapgqVLB2GKbabEuYBj32lFzZsZdDgxyhN0tywkFec96kqOjhDekFXCjmZIFNJi79AGt2S2bKN_mQYSDswmYTWbreviUh9488ksiUvMFTMGmBMYl7vxM2ljkBSlVoriy4EMqWLQZiIKU057OShPtOxSBnZvdy876X2gYm2i6HJipRUV2g_Szbxb4Hz3IlgLd24pQ0SlcuMpJLCIfHG9kDufvna1RLHRzsB4Rvct9s66BXqPBUl902hgCnHuuPx0nBeh8m9RnP8Nh6nVnVyynEi6tIDBdgIiIC84-ZGPCKLeF1-pON28T2UU12kmy0I7JJu-jtEs-Ck4EiiWppIxP1BUpdaKC0tpmKpGh2s8kKzuTgNiQdT45Y8r6JcxctXTGckMdh4lqvEtxGRG4Q4aVxFRGKgeiRcRo1x78hgtbxdW_D085uVlDtuZEOpLD_85i5PBPmRxMCWHY1xGmF6CB7yheSknWUJLG0sdk165CPybQfgulm0c1qw5x7ZarDtwzjnyk_FSw8N6QSuOGjU6NkH3ne-VSXYky4k7353aHH39yVancBDA%3D%3D)_ **

name: Teichert in the Trenches
subject: April 2026
body:
In the first three weeks of the conflict, I did 61 media interviews
---
| | | | | |
---
| | |
#### Teichert in the Trenches - April 2026
Welcome to this month’s installment of Teichert in the Trenches, a monthly newsletter highlighting a snippet of my attempts to maximize my impact on people and our great nation and to develop the leaders our nation needs!
My plans this month were a bit derailed by the war in the Middle East and my media engagements on that topic. I promise that this entire newsletter isn’t consumed by Iran subject matter, though it has been a bit all consuming. ** _Teichert in the News_**
---
| [ Teichert YouTube](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeFuBmP64SPACXkt7Xr7g/KccxVbqLCSYU)
---
The war dominated this month of news. In the first three weeks of the conflict, I did 61 media interviews (mostly on Fox News and NewsNation). Below I include a few that had special meaning to me. **HUMBLE REQUEST** : My YouTube Channel is linked above. Please subscribe, and if you are interested in a larger sample of interviews and videos, you can see them there.
| | | | [ ](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeG13EXv1dZJgdaBrW9hw/ypGQ5Yspz1mB)
---
Honoring our military fallen is necessary, appropriate, and powerful. I was honored to provide commentary immediately before the first Dignified Transfer ceremony of the conflict (w/ Alex Caprariello, March 7, NewsNation).
| | | | [ ](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeG7uggjyojTAjPUbUSIC/xW4byMSZwiPw)
---
I also provided my thoughts immediately after the ceremony as well (w/ Alex Caprariello, March 7, NewsNation).
| [ ](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeGEm8pYvztcepEnLSksS/NVqSq6aa7rTF)
---
Over the course of the conflict, I became the Fox & Friends commentator who provided my thoughts after the Pentagon press conferences (I considered Secretary Hegseth and General Caine my opening act 🤣). This is one from the second week of the war, following thoughts by former Secretary Pompeo. (w/ Fox & Friends Hosts, March 10, Fox News).
| [ ](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeGLdayNtB3m8v465R3Si/CWk8g1owjECo)
---
| | | This is one from the third week of the war, immediately after the press conference. Like the previous one, I enjoyed synthesizing the event real time to provide my perspective to the audience (w/ Fox & Friends Hosts, March 19, Fox News). ** _Thoughts by Teichert_**
---
| [ _In God I Trust_](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeGSV37CqMDvd0tOpPM2y/WvVMihbNuixv)
---
| | | _In God I Trust_ – available on Amazon – published on the first day of the war. Thus, I probably have fallen short in my promotional responsibilities (though book shout outs on some of the media engagements helped). Please rate, review, and share this birthday present to the United States of America with your network.
---
| | | | [ "Our Glorious Liberty Document"](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeGZMVG1nXO576ihZNedE/fmT1qYNV66Ri)
---
| | | I am honored to be a contributor to “Real Clear Politics” America Turns 250 series in 2026. This is my first of several contributions (March 2).
* “Douglass was an amazing 19th-century American orator, abolitionist, and woman’s suffragist. He was one of the greatest minds and speakers in American history, and his friendship molded the mind and shaped the perspective of President Abraham Lincoln. Based on his first-hand experience with the horrors of slavery, he offers a particularly unique view of the American experiment.”

---
| | | | [ "Air War Precedents"](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeGgDxOqkiYEbCY0JLxDU/pwc-XuYVLm1K)
---
| | | A huge thanks to “The Hill” for publishing a timely perspective on the air war in Iran as compared to the two modern air wars under Presidents Clinton and Obama (March 5).
* “With more than 50,000 servicemembers in harm’s way in the Middle East, amid a sustained air war that is only days old with a complex and multi-faceted rationale, those who have been quick to condemn the conflict would be wise to consider carefully the precedents of two other presidents not named Trump.”

---
| [ "Solving the Hormuz Problem"](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeGn5PXfhtiO5INJ3KFnk/hLE5w93k_c6X)
---
| | | Thanks to “Real Clear Politics” for publishing these thoughts about the proper strategy for the Strait of Hormuz (March 20).
* “The alternative strategies to open the Strait of Hormuz that are dominating the headlines certainly impose costs on Iran. Yet they also incur significant risk. It’s time to avoid such a balanced equation by imposing the right types of costs on Iran that are credible, effective, sustainable, and risk-minimizing. It is a way to solve the Hormuz problem that favors U.S. interests, meets our objectives, and preserves our resources.”

---
| [ Commanding Joint Base Andrews](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeGtwrgUf4sXZOCbnIYO0/a_Wq-XOhhO4q)
---
✈️ An inside look at the highest-visibility base on the planet - Joint Base Andrews!

🎉 I had a blast recording and listening to this one! A huge thanks to Collin Meisel for leading a great interview, and to War on the Rocks or regularly producing such superb products.

✅ Check this one out – you will thoroughly enjoy it and thoughtfully learn from it! SORRY, but this one requires a subscription.
|
---
📣 A lesson in history and heritage!

📘 I recently highlight thoughts from the book by Secretary Heather Wilson and General Dave Goldfein: “Get Back Up”. I included a great quote from these stellar leaders: “Studying history makes us smart, but heritage makes us proud.” What a great thought!

⛏️ I also recently posted about attending the Society of Mining, Metallurgy & Exploration Annual Conference. In that post, I promised a future thought about heritage and metallurgy. Here it is ...

🧠 As I was sitting in the conference, I realized that I had a metallurgy heritage. My granddad, who passed away in a lab accident when my dad was 5 years old, was a renowned metallurgist. In fact, like me he wrote three books as the sole author, and his fourth book as a co-author.

!!️ I also realized that I hadn’t adequately shared the stories I know about him with my kids.

💥 Doing so will make them proud. It is something I owe to my lineage to elevate our heritage! It's a lesson we can all implement in our lives and for our families.
| [ President Should Follow His Instincts, Not the Energy Markets](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/1t6Af4OiGsDhYHcZvsAP4JMNKzgWGO/F342sCehjHxJ)
---
| | | Another thanks to “Real Clear Politics” for publishing these thoughts about the proper strategy in Iran to reorient the prevailing strategic calculus (April 1).
* “A counter-energy strategy that heavily leverages diplomatic and economic instruments of power, backed by credible military threats, would remove the current sanctuary of the adversary. The president’s original threats against Iranian energy targets, driven by astute instincts, revealed an understanding of this important truth. It is now time to follow those instincts, immediately reversing the ongoing power imbalance and turning Iran’s one-sided sanctuary into a strategic liability.”

** _Teichert in the Trenches_**
---
| | |
---
| | |
---
| | | 🦀 It’s Maryland Day (March 25)!

💥 In 1634, the first European settlers landed on our shores.

✅ A great way to celebrate:

A few days ago, I had the honor to speak to an incredible group of Marylanders and American Patriots – the Upper Montgomery County Republican Women’s Club. Even with politics that are driving away our citizens, businesses, and jobs, there is HOPE! 🙏
---
| | | |
---
| | | **_Behind the Scenes_**
---
| | | |
---
📺 I’ve spent a lot of time here in the Fox News green room in DC this month, and wanted to pass on a peak behind the curtain. Charles Krauthammer continues to be a legend and source of inspiration!
|
---
When I do in-studio interviews, I need a bit of help getting rid of my cranial shine (I call it my halo). Thanks a ton to Jessica and the awesome team of makeup artists at NewsNation for doing whatever possible to make me look presentable.
|
---
| | | I spent a lot of time with NewsNation’s Leland Vittert this month on set (he’s probably sick of me by now). I took this from the anchor desk as he was doing his opening monologue – with my interview to follow. ** _My Bookshelf_**
---
| | | |
---
The Strategic Edge is “the point where thought and action meet, where insight into human behavior fuses with decisive leadership, and where every choice is tested against real outcomes ... the difference between leaders who thrive and those who stagnate comes down to three key factors: psychological mastery, decision-making clarity, and the ability to drive results under pressure.”
“You don’t scale by locking everything down. You scale by locking in what matters most – and liberating the rest.”
| | | |
---
I’m honored to have written the following endorsement for this powerful book: “Lee created this brilliant book by carefully listening to the experiences of others. Through his conversations, he has distilled the essence of resilience. It is not a single ingredient, however, but a menu of courses that can be deliberately layered into our lives to produce opportunity, growth, and discovery through the human experience that never follows a straight pathway towards success. The recipes you will find in this great book will provide needed nourishment for every season of life.”
|
---
Etty has been described as the young adult counterpart to Anne Frank – a Jewish young lady in Amsterdam who volunteered to serve in the Westerbork camp hospital before being shipped to Auschwitz where she was killed by her Nazi captors at 29 years old.
“Humiliation always involves two. The one who does the humiliating, and the one who allowed himself to be humiliated. If the second is missing, that is, if the passive party is immune to humiliation, then the humiliation vanishes into thin air. All that remains are vexatious measures that interfere with daily life but are not humiliations that weigh heavily on the soul ... They can’t do anything to us, they really can’t. They can harass us, they can rob us of our material goods, of our freedom of movement, but we ourselves forfeit our greatest assets by our misguided compliance ... our greatest injury is one we inflict upon ourselves.” ** _PLUS Posts for the Month_**

🌄 No Friends But The Mountains❗ ... and the United States and Israel !?️

💥 It was an honor during my time in Iraq to support the Kurdish government, the Kurdish people, and the Kurdish military. Here are a few pictures with their political and military leaders. An article of our active support to their military is included above as well. 🗞️

🔥 It was a true privilege to be in the center of support for a serious military force that has been credibly discussed in relation to the conflict in Iran.

🤣 Don’t laugh too hard at the cheesy mustache and the crooked hat in the one pic. There's a story there, I promise!

🔥 Even in a combat zone; even at the base of T-walls; even during a pandemic – always! Don’t miss an opportunity to help the people around you become the best possible versions of themselves.
| [ ](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/1t6Af4OiGsGRYYp8I0jw3rjLdvYwWm/pqijGIMOVPKH)

🧑🚀 🚀 ✈️ Monk and the Astronaut turns 20 years old!
💥 It’s pretty amazing that this episode aired twenty years ago – in March 2006 (thanks for the reminder James R!). Did you catch the amazing line that got me an IMDB page and (small) residual checks for life: “Stop, they’re friendlies, they’re friendlies!” I’m surprised it didn’t win me an Emmy! 🤣
🎥 You can find a link to this classic full segment below.
| [ ](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/1t6Af4OiGsGttH1YgMprE7nB1kXgEq/cuaaIB8-46zP)
---
**_Rejoin Opportunities_**
In April, I will be at the following events. See you there!?
* Attending Pastor Mike Burke’s Memorial Service (Columbia, MO, April 11)
* Officiating Chaplain Nathan Tubbs’ Promotion Ceremony (Cape Canaveral, FL, April 13)
* Keynote Speaking for the UNC Charlotte Innovator of the Year Banquet (Charlotte, NC, April 23)

**_Closing Thought_**
[ ](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/1t6Af4OiGsHMDzDz4ivmONr0PZWPwu/dAiD59B6rXiu)
Thoughts about service to our great nation!
**_Dad Jokes of the Month_**
My new pet peeve is people who confuse entomology and etymology.
I don’t have the words to tell you how much that bugs me!
BONUS (in honor of Maryland Day – March 25), and in case you missed it in one of the posts above:
Why didn’t the crab cross the road?
Because he always used the sidewalk.

clean_body: In the first three weeks of the conflict, I did 61 media interviews | | | | | | | | | Teichert in the Trenches - April 2026 Welcome to this month’s installment of Teichert in the Trenches, a monthly newsletter highlighting a snippet of my attempts to maximize my impact on people and our great nation and to develop the leaders our nation needs! My plans this month were a bit derailed by the war in the Middle East and my media engagements on that topic. I promise that this entire newsletter isn’t consumed by Iran subject matter, though it has been a bit all consuming. Teichert in the News | The war dominated this month of news. In the first three weeks of the conflict, I did 61 media interviews (mostly on Fox News and NewsNation). Below I include a few that had special meaning to me. HUMBLE REQUEST : My YouTube Channel is linked above. Please subscribe, and if you are interested in a larger sample of interviews and videos, you can see them there. | | | | Honoring our military fallen is necessary, appropriate, and powerful. I was honored to provide commentary immediately before the first Dignified Transfer ceremony of the conflict (w/ Alex Caprariello, March 7, NewsNation). | | | | I also provided my thoughts immediately after the ceremony as well (w/ Alex Caprariello, March 7, NewsNation). | Over the course of the conflict, I became the Fox & Friends commentator who provided my thoughts after the Pentagon press conferences (I considered Secretary Hegseth and General Caine my opening act 🤣). This is one from the second week of the war, following thoughts by former Secretary Pompeo. (w/ Fox & Friends Hosts, March 10, Fox News). | | | | This is one from the third week of the war, immediately after the press conference. Like the previous one, I enjoyed synthesizing the event real time to provide my perspective to the audience (w/ Fox & Friends Hosts, March 19, Fox News). Thoughts by Teichert | | | | In God I Trust – available on Amazon – published on the first day of the war. Thus, I probably have fallen short in my promotional responsibilities (though book shout outs on some of the media engagements helped). Please rate, review, and share this birthday present to the United States of America with your network. | | | | | | | I am honored to be a contributor to “Real Clear Politics” America Turns 250 series in 2026. This is my first of several contributions (March 2). “Douglass was an amazing 19th-century American orator, abolitionist, and woman’s suffragist. He was one of the greatest minds and speakers in American history, and his friendship molded the mind and shaped the perspective of President Abraham Lincoln. Based on his first-hand experience with the horrors of slavery, he offers a particularly unique view of the American experiment.” | | | | | | | A huge thanks to “The Hill” for publishing a timely perspective on the air war in Iran as compared to the two modern air wars under Presidents Clinton and Obama (March 5). “With more than 50,000 servicemembers in harm’s way in the Middle East, amid a sustained air war that is only days old with a complex and multi-faceted rationale, those who have been quick to condemn the conflict would be wise to consider carefully the precedents of two other presidents not named Trump.” | | | | Thanks to “Real Clear Politics” for publishing these thoughts about the proper strategy for the Strait of Hormuz (March 20). “The alternative strategies to open the Strait of Hormuz that are dominating the headlines certainly impose costs on Iran. Yet they also incur significant risk. It’s time to avoid such a balanced equation by imposing the right types of costs on Iran that are credible, effective, sustainable, and risk-minimizing. It is a way to solve the Hormuz problem that favors U.S. interests, meets our objectives, and preserves our resources.” | ✈️ An inside look at the highest-visibility base on the planet - Joint Base Andrews! 🎉 I had a blast recording and listening to this one! A huge thanks to Collin Meisel for leading a great interview, and to War on the Rocks or regularly producing such superb products. ✅ Check this one out – you will thoroughly enjoy it and thoughtfully learn from it! SORRY, but this one requires a subscription. | 📣 A lesson in history and heritage! 📘 I recently highlight thoughts from the book by Secretary Heather Wilson and General Dave Goldfein: “Get Back Up”. I included a great quote from these stellar leaders: “Studying history makes us smart, but heritage makes us proud.” What a great thought! ⛏️ I also recently posted about attending the Society of Mining, Metallurgy & Exploration Annual Conference. In that post, I promised a future thought about heritage and metallurgy. Here it is ... 🧠 As I was sitting in the conference, I realized that I had a metallurgy heritage. My granddad, who passed away in a lab accident when my dad was 5 years old, was a renowned metallurgist. In fact, like me he wrote three books as the sole author, and his fourth book as a co-author. !!️ I also realized that I hadn’t adequately shared the stories I know about him with my kids. 💥 Doing so will make them proud. It is something I owe to my lineage to elevate our heritage! It's a lesson we can all implement in our lives and for our families. | | | | Another thanks to “Real Clear Politics” for publishing these thoughts about the proper strategy in Iran to reorient the prevailing strategic calculus (April 1). “A counter-energy strategy that heavily leverages diplomatic and economic instruments of power, backed by credible military threats, would remove the current sanctuary of the adversary. The president’s original threats against Iranian energy targets, driven by astute instincts, revealed an understanding of this important truth. It is now time to follow those instincts, immediately reversing the ongoing power imbalance and turning Iran’s one-sided sanctuary into a strategic liability.” Teichert in the Trenches | | | | | | | | | 🦀 It’s Maryland Day (March 25)! 💥 In 1634, the first European settlers landed on our shores. ✅ A great way to celebrate: A few days ago, I had the honor to speak to an incredible group of Marylanders and American Patriots – the Upper Montgomery County Republican Women’s Club. Even with politics that are driving away our citizens, businesses, and jobs, there is HOPE! 🙏 | | | | | | | Behind the Scenes | | | | 📺 I’ve spent a lot of time here in the Fox News green room in DC this month, and wanted to pass on a peak behind the curtain. Charles Krauthammer continues to be a legend and source of inspiration! | When I do in-studio interviews, I need a bit of help getting rid of my cranial shine (I call it my halo). Thanks a ton to Jessica and the awesome team of makeup artists at NewsNation for doing whatever possible to make me look presentable. | | | | I spent a lot of time with NewsNation’s Leland Vittert this month on set (he’s probably sick of me by now). I took this from the anchor desk as he was doing his opening monologue – with my interview to follow. My Bookshelf | | | | The Strategic Edge is “the point where thought and action meet, where insight into human behavior fuses with decisive leadership, and where every choice is tested against real outcomes ... the difference between leaders who thrive and those who stagnate comes down to three key factors: psychological mastery, decision-making clarity, and the ability to drive results under pressure.” “You don’t scale by locking everything down. You scale by locking in what matters most – and liberating the rest.” | | | | I’m honored to have written the following endorsement for this powerful book: “Lee created this brilliant book by carefully listening to the experiences of others. Through his conversations, he has distilled the essence of resilience. It is not a single ingredient, however, but a menu of courses that can be deliberately layered into our lives to produce opportunity, growth, and discovery through the human experience that never follows a straight pathway towards success. The recipes you will find in this great book will provide needed nourishment for every season of life.” | Etty has been described as the young adult counterpart to Anne Frank – a Jewish young lady in Amsterdam who volunteered to serve in the Westerbork camp hospital before being shipped to Auschwitz where she was killed by her Nazi captors at 29 years old. “Humiliation always involves two. name: Patrick J. Murphy
subject: A Message for Holy Week
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP25/1/106220/images/PJM_Logo.png]
Holy week is upon us. If you're observing, I wish you a blessed Good Friday and a joyful Easter Sunday ahead.
This time of year has always been a core part of my life. I grew up Catholic at St. Anselm’s Parish in Northeast Philly, raised by a mother who had been in the convent before she married my dad. As you can imagine, faith was something that was deeply baked into our life and remains to this day.
This year for Lent, I gave up alcohol - Forty days is a long time to go without something you reach for automatically after a long day or long travel. That's precisely the point. Discomfort is the practice of discipline. You learn what you actually depend on when you remove it.
The story of Easter moves through betrayal, trial, suffering, and loss over the course of a week before it gets anywhere near the joy of Sunday morning. That full arc is what gives the season its power. You don't get to skip to the good part without first experiencing the emotional depth that precedes it. It’s why I’ve been sharing some of my not-so-great stories of my journey (rejected twice by Notre Dame, unemployed for five months after the Army, etc).
Jesus rising from death is a sequence that speaks to something beyond any single faith tradition. Everyone has experienced some kind of loss or setback. Veterans I served with in Iraq know that feeling in ways that don't have easy words - so most keep it inside. A lot of them came home and found their version of Good Friday — the long, uncertain stretch that includes a horribly wicked death. What Easter offers, at its core, is the insistence that Good Friday is not the end of the story. That something can be restored, even after it looks finished.
It is always darkest before dawn.
This year, Easter and Passover overlap, which is always a reminder that the two holidays share the same roots. The Last Supper, after all, was a Passover Seder. Both holidays ask the same founding question: what does it mean to be led out of darkness into liberation. I love when religious - as well as political leaders - find unity in our shared purpose.
Last week, I had the privilege of speaking at Notre Dame Law School's Military and Veteran Law Clinic’s first annual symposium. You can find my speech here [https://substack.com/home/post/p-192627911] . As a proud Irish Catholic who has attended Mass almost every Sunday since childhood, speaking at Notre Dame was extremely meaningful to me. I didn’t discuss the first American Pope - Pope Leo’s sermon to our political leaders about peace and finding a way to end our war of choice - but it weighed heavily on the student veterans I talked to privately.
I hope you find something this weekend worth holding onto. A moment of stillness. A meal with people you love. A reminder that the people around you are worth showing up for.
From my family to yours, Happy Easter. He is risen!
- Patrick
P.S.Thank you reading our newsletter. For more updates, please follow on Instagram [https://bit.ly/47s6mRv] and subscribe to my best-selling (31st in country) Substack [https://bit.ly/4te4VOV] for our weekly geopolitical and business analysis. Most importantly, please keep our troops and everyone in the region in your nightly prayers.

If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/JcTKjLm2l0SduvIvv8SeUg2?unsubscribedata=rgE6tbfbmYzKPbYKRNJLb5GO6iUMCAb1xE1VNFhgGjZPcaBBGs96xT%2FK7Z3r%2B6QMxjyK95BgnUqciSfLMkE089sjhwVCXZkULPtKZfK64ttGE893ewsvDRw6Ai8KQFEn5S5e1wIjrS355Xhownp3IGOxIroUIcdhU6I%2Bi5G%2BdpRYkNZBqY8r%2FSXgsv49SKt%2Bh4XKAcGJieWEVglQ7ct2NGSRNtYVgBNG4CjBy5VdlM%2B0MuCQRaWg3EJVrWBjDJfoXO5OytmC3M7oqO0%2FiAunuGcNXqW8t3D1tBfdJc%2FG0Jjb81ILK8QvFOyYaBCI27LSmvAPl974d2kfRPmrM%2BhR%2BAz8iJBiImkoUUlNmhMMCOEfD0OTdmj8tCnej3OvxW81x93UETEIZC3%2FEN18PSAMZjdz7XagITQZMXGhF5J7XXK%2BHwEzM0e2UUVhUj6Uvwjn .

name: Yen Bailey
subject: They have PACs and special interest money. We have you.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY YEN BAILEY FOR CONGRESS
body:
| | | | |
---
[|
---](https://yenbaileyforcongress.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=642870bb478010796c7288d8f&id=f257fdc061&e=58216231a8)
| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |

Friends,Your support right now is more important than ever.
This quarter, our campaign knocked on hundreds of doors across the district, stood with the community at No Kings Day protests, petitioned, tabled, and brought dozens of new volunteers and interns onto the team. None of it could happen without people like you.
While our opponents are bankrolled by corporate PACs and big money, we are building this campaign the grassroots way, powered by the community, for the community.We are just hours from closing out the quarter and $5,000 short of our fundraising goal. Hitting that number is what allows me to keep fighting! Fighting to lower costs for families, to bring good jobs to North Florida, improve healthcare, protect seniors, support veterans, and fix our broken immigration system.
A donation of just $26 by midnight tonight puts us over the finish line. Please don’t wait.
---
| [CONTRIBUTE $26 NOW](https://yenbaileyforcongress.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=642870bb478010796c7288d8f&id=7ccae0472c&e=58216231a8)
---
| |

Thank you, I truly cannot do this without you.
With Appreciation,Yen Bailey
---
|
---
| | | | | | | Yen Bailey’s journey began with her parents, who fled war-torn Vietnam. Her father, a South Vietnamese Air Force pilot, bravely escaped the fall of Saigon by flying with his crew and dozens of Vietnamese refugees. They settled in Destin, Florida, where Yen and her brother were born and raised. Witnessing her parents' sacrifices and resilience deeply influenced Yen, inspiring her to pursue a career in law and public service.

[ ](https://yenbaileyforcongress.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=642870bb478010796c7288d8f&id=d9ac89d009&e=58216231a8)

A Tallahassee-based attorney and Tulane University and Florida State University Law School graduate, Yen has dedicated her career to public service. She served as a Senior Attorney for the Guardian ad Litem Program and spent over 13 years as an ethics attorney at The Florida Bar. Committed to her family, Yen stepped away from her law career at the Bar to care for her elderly father.

Yen is a proud wife and mother. She cherishes time with her husband, Steve Bailey, a lawyer and professor at Florida State University and former Army Green Beret, and their two teenage sons.

Yen’s dedication to civic engagement led her to co-found Every Vote Florida, a nonprofit voter outreach organization, where she served as Vice President. She also served on the board of Friends of the LeRoy Collins Public Library System, promoting literacy and supporting public libraries in Leon County.

Yen is running for office to address the needs and challenges of the average Floridian in the panhandle. For years, many in the district have felt neglected and left behind by their Representatives, and Yen wants to be a voice for all in Washington D.C.

With her extensive background in public service and dedication to the community, Yen Bailey offers a fresh perspective for District 2. Her journey from the daughter of immigrants to a congressional candidate embodies the American dream and the power of perseverance.

**Prefer to donate via mail? Address a check to:**
P.O. Box 12699
Tallahassee, FL 32317

name: Eric Burlison for Congress
subject: The Senate ran. I need your help.
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Eric Burlison for Congress]()
body:
This fight takes resources. I can't do it alone.

| | | | | [|
---](https://ericburlisonforcongress.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b6500a115da3c4344522b1e48&id=e8439881b0&e=5f9dc01b8c)
| | Peter ,

This week I told you about the Senate's midnight betrayal, how they defunded ICE, Border Patrol, and the HSI team that fights child sex trafficking, then left town for Easter while the rest of us stayed to fight.I want you to know where things stand right now.Speaker Johnson is sending the bill back. The Freedom Caucus is united. We're not leaving until this gets fixed. The men and women who protect our borders and rescue children from trafficking deserve to be funded, and we're going to make sure they are.Here's what I need you to understand. I'm going to keep fighting this fight regardless. That's my job. But in Congress, you run for re-election every two years. That means the people who want me out of this seat — the ones who'd rather have someone who goes along and gets along — they never stop coming.If you want someone in Congress who will stay on a Friday night and fight for the people the Senate left behind, I'm asking you to stand with me. Your support keeps me in this fight for the long haul.[**> >[Help Me Fight!]<<**](https://ericburlisonforcongress.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b6500a115da3c4344522b1e48&id=ead6f35698&e=5f9dc01b8c)We're not backing down. Thank you for having my back.Still fighting,

Trump cut $1.1 billion in public broadcasting funds, threatening access to NPR and PBS stations, particularly in rural communities.

For many Americans, these stations are a lifeline to local news, public affairs, and trusted information about what’s happening in their communities and government.

Now, with these cuts threatening to eliminate or reduce access altogether, we need to understand how people are being affected.

**That’s why we’re launching a nationwide survey to hear directly from folks like you. We’re hoping to receive 1,000 responses, and friend, you’ve been selected to represent PA:** | **Survey Poll:**

name: Save the Persecuted Christians
subject: A brutal new wave of slaughter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Save the Persecuted Christians
body:
Just days ago, during Palm Sunday week, terrorists unleashed horror after horror on defenseless Christian communities.

| | | | [](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zkeh8a04aa5bcl0nqdbeae6vjqt2pe6jedm9pso8_rp22sh2s8i6epr5c9j69p33c5h2bs)
---
Friend, This Easter, as we rejoice that He is risen, our brothers and sisters in Christ in Nigeria are weeping over fresh graves and tending to the wounded from a brutal new wave of jihadist slaughter. **Just days ago, during Palm Sunday week, terrorists unleashed horror after horror on defenseless Christian communities.** Gunmen stormed neighborhoods, opening fire on families gathered after worship — slaughtering dozens in the streets. **At least 28 believers cut down in one community alone.** That same night, attackers raided a Christian wedding celebration, **mowing down 13 guests and dragging others into the darkness.** Across multiple attacks this week, **nearly 100 precious souls were killed** — fathers, mothers, young believers, entire families wiped out _simply for loving Jesus._ The volume of this evil is staggering. One massacre after another. Blood in the streets. Widows left sobbing. Children orphaned overnight. Survivors maimed for life, crying out in agony while far too many across the world look away. **Easter is one of the most dangerous times of the year for persecuted Christians around the globe.** When the Church gathers visibly to proclaim the risen Savior — singing, worshiping, remembering the cross — the enemy strikes with fury. Year after year, hundreds die in Easter attacks. **This Palm Sunday massacre is a heartbreaking reminder that for millions, Holy Week means terror, not triumph.** Yet amid the bloodshed, they still refuse to deny Christ. Their unshakeable faith in the empty tomb humbles and convicts us. **Here at Save the Persecuted Christians, the cries of these suffering saints demand our immediate response. We ended Q1 short of our goal, and the needs are exploding — more widows, more orphans, more displaced families facing starvation and fear.**
---
[SAVE CHRISTIANS](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z3jrhp1sgotbblr3g8n1nvdtf8fc99os55ormf48_rp22sh2s8i6epr5c9j69p33c5h2bs)
---
These precious believers desperately need our prayers. But beyond that they need a loud, persistent voice raised on their behalf — exposing the evil to the world, rallying the global Church to stand with them, and pressing leaders in D.C. and abroad to take real action that stops the slaughter and protects the vulnerable.

**[Your gift today will fuel our advocacy, amplify their stories, open doors with policymakers, and send a powerful message to the persecuted: You are seen. You are not alone. The risen Christ stands with you — and so do we.](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z1q70o1eaels44b0sv83i3l6ibvrbgoplnh323v8_rp22sh2s8i6epr5c9j69p33c5h2bs)**

_Will you act now?_ Every hour we hesitate, more blood may be shed. More families shattered. Your generosity right now declares: The risen Christ has overcome the grave — and we will not abandon His suffering Church.

He is risen. Let’s act like it — urgently, sacrificially, with hearts fully engaged.

In faith and urgent prayer, Dede Laugesen
President & CEO
Save the Persecuted Christians
---
---

body:
| | | | | [](https://6s79z9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001XuqRcr_sEm9JN4aS4fbG8fpFEaGHMLDdc3FHHujSBtE1Jyjw39s43D5rm_nc1zooJBoHSr1gfjoBFujWYnlxAaIpNJ15B_ZfyPNsmXuyf_TlXEK9phW7RW5bI1_PgE-mo82tlyXkFvQJI85r5Z-JJCDWgWUsRHD_HXZ7-JLghlV_VdJMFN8tQm7-bhg2wHwRHireuTZCd0sps5_umr7BuQ==&c=CqZjR0tXmAc2cOgWZIx_Ph5EpHnFd-BY5AYNVYGnv1CaIk7jnYoZuQ==&ch=_hiVbsM436ho-4h9bw-0eM1hksH1VQmCnZ89HGXHJw2tZ9mvNocPAg==)
---
Peter,
Ladies and gentlemen, welcome to the main event.
In this corner: MAGA Republicans, armed with gerrymandered maps in Texas, Florida, North Carolina, Missouri, and Indiana. They came out swinging, trying to rig enough seats to lock in one-party rule for a generation.
In the other corner: Grassroots Democrats, fighting back state by state to level the playing field and save democracy.
Twelve rounds. Winner takes the House. Let's break down the scorecard. ROUNDS 1-3: MAGA LANDS EARLY BLOWS 🥊
Texas gerrymandered 12+ new GOP seats. Florida followed with 4 more. North Carolina locked in their maps. MAGA came out aggressive, trying to win by knockout before Democrats even got off the ropes.
The crowd went quiet. The pundits declared it over. "Democrats can't recover from this," they said.
ROUNDS 4-6: DEMOCRATS FIRE BACK 💥
California landed a MASSIVE counterpunch with Prop 50--5 GOP seats eliminated. The Supreme Court let it stand. MAGA staggered.
New York followed with a precision strike--last GOP seat in the state GONE.
Virginia got cleared by their Supreme Court to redistrict and flip 4 more seats.
Suddenly, the fight is EVEN.
ROUNDS 7-11: TRADING BLOWS
Both sides are landing hits. MAGA's trying to gerrymander Indiana and gut the Voting Rights Act. Democrats are organizing in Maryland and rallying grassroots donors.
The judges are split. The crowd is on its feet. Nobody knows who's ahead on points.
ROUND 12: COLORADO STEPS INTO THE RING 🔥
This is it. The final round. The knockout opportunity.
Colorado Draws the Line is the uppercut that MAGA never saw coming. We're flipping 3-4 seats--potentially including Lauren Boebert's--and tipping the entire fight in Democrats' favor.
MAGA can't absorb this hit. They're already reeling from California and New York. Their majority is razor-thin. Their coalition is fracturing. One more solid punch and they're DOWN.
Colorado is that punch.
But here's the thing about championship fights: you don't win by throwing punches--you win by LANDING them.
And landing this punch requires POWER. Resources. Grassroots energy. Donors stepping up when the bell rings for the final round.
[Will you chip in $50, $30, $15, or whatever you can spare to help Colorado deliver the knockout blow that ends MAGA's rigging scheme once and for all?](https://6s79z9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001XuqRcr_sEm9JN4aS4fbG8fpFEaGHMLDdc3FHHujSBtE1Jyjw39s43D5rm_nc1zooseLCM5j-_-E9a6-u6g-c7IBRzqECrHr2TsVojM6m91kOAqhnojdDXzXHb7rrOH0lRNl3BbNHEcefhPQzUV6jxd8KDTo1_0awsVj0maJDd9IpB9-CveMy3wQ4tPKNG3tkyy6aOYB86OwmT18wiGg6ogET8a2MAX4y&c=CqZjR0tXmAc2cOgWZIx_Ph5EpHnFd-BY5AYNVYGnv1CaIk7jnYoZuQ==&ch=_hiVbsM436ho-4h9bw-0eM1hksH1VQmCnZ89HGXHJw2tZ9mvNocPAg==)

| Hi Peter,

Recently, I voted YES on Virginia’s redistricting referendum.
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128692902/612077515/-1248433231?refcode=BLU_EM_UFVF_2026-04-03_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNDVjMmI1NzctNjgyZi1mMTExLTlhNDgtMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=4fXEc-qZ4UuYrWVi0q0OncRx7P2He_TkuNdbAcf5UIw=)
Across the country, we’re seeing states cater to a President who says he’s “entitled” to more Republican Congressional seats before this year’s midterms. It’s an undemocratic and desperate attempt to lock in power.

**But right now, Virginia has the opportunity to take action in response. On April 21, we vote on a referendum to give the people – not politicians – the right to decide what comes next.**

Our approach is different from what we’re seeing coming out of some Republican states. In Virginia, this referendum would be temporary. It’s directly in response to what other states decide to do. And, most importantly, it preserves Virginia’s bipartisan redistricting process into the future.

[_**At this extraordinary moment, I hope you’ll support our efforts to support efforts like Virginia’s redistricting referendum. If you’re able, please make a donation to Virginians for Fair Elections today.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128692906/612077519/-1248433231?refcode=BLU_EM_UFVF_2026-04-03_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNDVjMmI1NzctNjgyZi1mMTExLTlhNDgtMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=4fXEc-qZ4UuYrWVi0q0OncRx7P2He_TkuNdbAcf5UIw=)

clean_body: Across the country, we’re seeing states cater to a President who says he’s “entitled” to more Republican Congressional seats before this year’s midterms. | = | Hi Peter, Recently, I voted YES on Virginia’s redistricting referendum. = Across the country, we’re seeing states cater to a President who says he’s “entitled” to more Republican Congressional seats before this year’s midterms. It’s an undemocratic and desperate attempt to lock in power. But right now, Virginia has the opportunity to take action in response. On April 21, we vote on a referendum to give the people – not politicians – the right to decide what comes next. Our approach is different from what we’re seeing coming out of some Republican states. In Virginia, this referendum would be temporary. It’s directly in response to what other states decide to do. And, most importantly, it preserves Virginia’s bipartisan redistricting process into the future. = | = Thank you, Governor Abigail Spanberger | | = Paid for by United for Virginia's Future PAC United for Virginia's Future PAC P.O. Box 3121 Glen Allen, VA 23058 United States *

body:
| | | Y'all... Ohio and Florida.**Ohio and freakin' Florida!**

As pissed as I am that Trump and his followers are back in charge, they could be giving us two HUGE chances to take back the Senate.

Putting JD Vance's and Marco Rubio's seats up for grabs in special elections? I mean, they might as well be handing us a personalized invitation to get right back in this!

And you know what? We've gotta reply HELL YES! If you agree, **[chip in $7 today to fight for every seat and say "sorry Donald Trump, but we're gonna win back the Senate!!" **So are we gonna do this or what?!** We could be looking at a once‐in‐a‐presidency chance to STOP Trump, but it'll ONLY work if we take advantage. **We need all hands on deck, so don't wait another moment:**

subject: The clock is running out for Dems in WI
body:
The latest Marquette Law School polling shows 53% of voters are still undecided—with less than a week to go.

---
| | Friend,
588,088 voters.
That's our Get Out the Vote (GOTV) universe for the Wisconsin Supreme Court election this Tuesday.
Reaching them is critical. And right now, we're coming up short. The latest Marquette Law School polling shows 53% of voters are still undecided--with less than a week to go.
---
| [](https://zzejj9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001kHiILzRvS-spdhHtBrYwS26fHPkOqs_U-eJUAdNZrkD7hbJAk7XlUfJAkYP-n_gzfgp81sjNmAHdXR_1vO0jsVqCf0lmLcCNBIRwa6az4b3W_Y0YfHsTIWfj9PcC3zyQBxfj3y9n1-Inm70e9nPqbB-fRDUzx6QKoH9Ed4A-rHXiHZkmtTIDg9HYxL88OQyqQYVgU933NfJ2gChwmwZOeq7oINHFgtwgYFK1mm2n9nIERIasfZ5oVmofS_VQbZ4INqNCGBCT5o8=&c=s5aNfFUX_ZsOawOuK-3lLAq4VL3WBdCBjXM07olbA6fg2eB8rfBfOg==&ch=-j1xXTv-oEIYjt5drhNekRrhXtmTotRpjn1CaZf4PEhfoBY0PH9-cQ==)
---
| Why? Because they haven't heard enough about the candidates.
That's the gap MAGA is counting on. And it's the gap we are working around the clock to close.
Defend Our Courts is already reaching voters across Wisconsin--having direct conversations to break through the noise and make sure people understand what's at stake.
We're telling voters the truth about Judge Chris Taylor:

* Her record standing up for everyday Wisconsinites
* The support of 150+ current and former judges across the state
* Her work with Planned Parenthood defending reproductive freedom

And we're making sure voters know about her opponent, MAGA Maria Lazar, too:
* Backed by election deniers
* Supports restrictive voting laws
* Aligned with the same movement that pushed the 1849 abortion ban
* Connected to the Federalist Society network that helped deliver Trump's Supreme Court majority

This is the choice. And with Election Day just days away, we cannot afford to lose.
Between now and Tuesday, we are pushing to reach all of our targeted voters multiple times, but to fully execute our plan, we need to raise $50,000 immediately.
If we fall short, we leave voters in the dark. If we hit this goal, we can close the gap and win. [Can you chip in $50, $25, or more right now to help us reach these voters and lock in a pro-democracy majority on Wisconsin's Supreme Court through 2030?](https://zzejj9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001kHiILzRvS-spdhHtBrYwS26fHPkOqs_U-eJUAdNZrkD7hbJAk7XlUfJAkYP-n_gzfgp81sjNmAHdXR_1vO0jsVqCf0lmLcCNBIRwa6az4b3W_Y0YfHsTIWfj9PcC3zyQBxfj3y9n1-Inm70e9nPqbB-fRDUzx6QKoH9Ed4A-rHXiHZkmtTIDg9HYxL88OQyqQYVgU933NfJ2gChwmwZOeq7oINHFgtwgYFK1mm2n9nIERIasfZ5oVu8Y2ikzzXWIf8sWuJ1MGrM=&c=s5aNfFUX_ZsOawOuK-3lLAq4VL3WBdCBjXM07olbA6fg2eB8rfBfOg==&ch=-j1xXTv-oEIYjt5drhNekRrhXtmTotRpjn1CaZf4PEhfoBY0PH9-cQ==)

clean_body: The latest Marquette Law School polling shows 53% of voters are still undecided—with less than a week to go. | | | | If you believe you've received this email in error, you may unsubscribe below. | | | | | == | | Friend, 588,088 voters. That's our Get Out the Vote (GOTV) universe for the Wisconsin Supreme Court election this Tuesday. Reaching them is critical. And right now, we're coming up short. The latest Marquette Law School polling shows 53% of voters are still undecided--with less than a week to go. | == | Why? Because they haven't heard enough about the candidates. That's the gap MAGA is counting on. And it's the gap we are working around the clock to close. Defend Our Courts is already reaching voters across Wisconsin--having direct conversations to break through the noise and make sure people understand what's at stake. We're telling voters the truth about Judge Chris Taylor: Her record standing up for everyday Wisconsinites The support of 150+ current and former judges across the state Her work with Planned Parenthood defending reproductive freedom And we're making sure voters know about her opponent, MAGA Maria Lazar, too: Backed by election deniers Supports restrictive voting laws Aligned with the same movement that pushed the 1849 abortion ban * Connected to the Federalist Society network that helped deliver Trump's Supreme Court majority This is the choice. And with Election Day just days away, we cannot afford to lose. Between now and Tuesday, we are pushing to reach all of our targeted voters multiple times, but to fully execute our plan, we need to raise $50,000 immediately. If we fall short, we leave voters in the dark. If we hit this goal, we can close the gap and win. == | | | | | == | == | == | == | Trump and MAGA are already moving to control who can vote and who can't. This is where we stop them. --Team Defend our Courts | | == | Defend Our Courts | P.O. Box 45569 | Philadelphia, PA 19149 US | | | | | |

* * *
# **Rep. Tim Burchett says Americans would be "up at night worrying" if they knew what he's been briefed on about extraterrestrial life**
Rep. Tim Burchett **[told Newsmax](https://substack.com/redirect/17767c07-9471-47a4-a534-7d35344c846d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** that Americans would be "up at night, worrying about, thinking about this stuff" if the government released what he's seen, saying "I've been briefed by just about every alphabet agency there is." The House Task Force on the Declassification of Federal Secrets member recalled being briefed two weeks ago "on an issue" about extraterrestrial life that "would have set the Earth on fire" and "unglued" the country if released, adding "they would demand answers."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/279408a6-6a7d-4bab-9aa4-fba0a15b1ca7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Why it matters:** When shown a clip of former Rep. Matt Gaetz alleging knowledge of alien-human hybrid breeding programs, Burchett said "I'm still a member of Congress, so I can't really comment too much on what Matt said," adding he was "being 100% serious." He revealed being told by "some arrogant, unelected bureaucrat that the president is on a need-to-know basis," saying "I think that says everything it needs to be said about what's going on."

[| | | | | Defiant L's
---
@DefiantLs
Rep. Tim Burchett speaks about "aliens" "If people saw what I've seen, it would've set the Earth on fire!" "I'm not suicidal."
| 12:10 PM * Apr 2, 2026 * 43.8K Views50 Replies * 64 Reposts * 447 Likes
---](https://substack.com/redirect/17767c07-9471-47a4-a534-7d35344c846d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **The disappearances:** Burchett weighed in on the "alarming string of disappearances and deaths among the country's top researchers," including retired Air Force Gen. William McCasland in February and rocket scientist Monica Reza eight months earlier, saying "there are no coincidences in this town. These folks have disappeared or died mysteriously," adding "for the record, I'm not suicidal, and I don't take risks."

* * *
# **Acting AG Todd Blanche denies Trump fired Pam Bondi over Epstein files handling**
Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche **[denied reports that President Trump fired Pam Bondi over her handling of Epstein files](https://substack.com/redirect/a48e399f-0216-4499-889a-99f252bd969c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , telling "Jesse Watters Primetime" Thursday "I have never heard President Trump say that the attorney general was -- that anything that happened to her -- had anything to do with the Epstein files." Blanche also dismissed a Daily Mail report claiming Trump believed Bondi tipped off Rep. Eric Swalwell about the FBI's plan to release files on Chinese spy Christine Fang, saying "I would not believe that for a second -- absolutely, positively not."
[| | | | | Jesse Watters
---
@JesseBWatters
🚨 TODD BLANCHE REPLACES PAM BONDI AS ACTING ATTORNEY GENERAL 🚨 Blanche TORCHES the RUMORS about BONDI: "Absolutely, positively NOT" ... "she MADE OUR COUNTRY SAFER"
| 12:54 AM * Apr 3, 2026 * 107K Views255 Replies * 181 Reposts * 1.09K Likes
---](https://substack.com/redirect/618e304b-7e49-424f-9ff2-108c371a368a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Why it matters:** Bondi's ousting marked Trump's second Cabinet shakeup in one month after firing DHS Secretary Kristi Noem on March 5. Blanche said he and Bondi handled the Epstein assignment and the Justice Department "has now released all the files with respect to the Epstein saga," making "every single congressman, senator available to come and see any document redacted, unredacted," adding "to the extent that the Epstein files was a part of the past year of this Justice Department, it should not be a part of anything going forward."
* **What 's next:** Trump confirmed Bondi's ousting on Truth Social, calling her a "Great American Patriot" and saying she will transition to the private sector, while calling Blanche a "respected Legal Mind" to intermittently serve as AG until a permanent replacement is found. EPA Director Lee Zeldin is reportedly being considered to replace Bondi, with discussions about the transition taking place during a Tuesday White House meeting.

| 7:03 PM * Apr 2, 2026 * 3.72M Views23.1K Replies * 5.39K Reposts * 41K Likes
---](https://substack.com/redirect/61b3c9fc-e0aa-4be8-9f61-7100e57d430b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
# **US payrolls surge by 178,000 in March, smashing forecast of 59,000 as unemployment falls to 4.3%**
Employment in the United States **[expanded by 178,000 in March](https://substack.com/redirect/6724a2fa-b66a-4766-9a59-565123cd904d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** and the unemployment rate fell to 4.3%, smashing economists' forecast of 59,000 jobs added with unemployment holding at 4.4%.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6724a2fa-b66a-4766-9a59-565123cd904d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Where are the new jobs?:** Healthcare led gains with 76,000 workers added after February's large strike pulled the prior month's estimate down to a revised decline of 133,000, while construction jumped 26,000, transportation and warehousing expanded 21,000, and manufacturing added 15,000.
* **Why it matters:** The Trump administration's efforts to shrink federal government and "reprivatize" the economy continued, with government payrolls declining 18,000--down 355,000 or 11.8% since the Biden peak in October 2024--while private employment rose 186,000.
* **The wages:** Average hourly earnings for all employees rose 9 cents or 0.2% to $37.38, with annual growth of 3.5% beating inflation, while production and nonsupervisory employees' earnings edged up 5 cents to $32.07. The average work week inched down to 34.2 hours from 34.3, and labor force participation ticked down from 62% to 61.9%.

Hey Derek, it’s Shapiro HQ. body:
Justice still demands action | [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128698968/612091422/-1977837920?refcode=4.03.26_MLK&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODg3MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YmVjOThjNi02ZDJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=N_jXvKlkYSOGXAZfn-HoMksBzwwsSc3viXF_semble0=&emci=f1b57423-022e-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&emdi=5bec98c6-6d2f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&ceid=53531271)
---
Friend, This Saturday marks the **58th anniversary of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.’s assassination in Memphis, TN,** where his fight for equality and dignity for all was met with derision, hate, and violence. The world lost a great leader that day, but _Dr. King’s message could never be silenced by a bullet._ It lives on within each of us, and his struggle, our struggle, continues. Today, Memphis and Tennessee _still_ face challenges that echo the same struggles confronted by Dr. King and his movement. Families still struggle to make ends meet, the rights of women are under constant threat, and we are entrenched in a growing foriegn war with no end in sight. The recent deployment of the National Guard troops to Memphis, ongoing ICE raids targeting immigrant families, and police violence against peaceful demonstrators, like at Memphis’s recent No Kings Rally, all remind us that Dr. King’s fight for justice is far from over. **As Governor, Jerri Green will build on Dr. King's legacy** , standing firmly on the side of fairness and equality: ensuring all Tennesseans have access to affordable healthcare, fighting for a living wage, defending immigrant communities from fear and persecution, and advancing policies that make our communities safer while upholding human dignity and civil rights. _**Dr. King taught us that injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere.**_ At a time when some seek to rewrite history, it is more important than ever that we do more than remember. __Honoring his legacy means committing ourselves to the work_ :_ fighting poverty, protecting our communities, uplifting marginalized voices, and holding our elected leaders accountable. _**Together, we can ensure that Memphis and all of Tennessee are places where everyone is treated with dignity, respect, and fairness.**_ | | [FUEL OUR MOVEMENT](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128698969/612091424/-843357993?refcode=4.03.26_MLK&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODg3MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YmVjOThjNi02ZDJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=N_jXvKlkYSOGXAZfn-HoMksBzwwsSc3viXF_semble0=)
---
In Solidarity,
Team Green
Jerri Green for Governor

name: The New York Sun
subject: The New York Times Soft-Pedals Antisemitic Terror
body:
The New York Sun told you the truth.

| | | [](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=fa9a72ad36b34d85978c405a0ab77ede&_e=JhVLDxUNwCev6kRqTQYj7-cpxrOIc0WFXiws9Dy-DPLitlolD_EFgMS2cv5-hVQAKW86TOLnBdl_UpKpy9oQ1NXIE0WFYtYUeZRvJHSSaYMUsvSAoNXWkdlS3zomOHvKGbb0zLi-Nqd3qzz42ADXQjme2iChHTRbPGIFJnsRdx3xmj5FljMEgmn9Se3R_r0DER3tTD3mwVzkmL5sIOGagGjbeUNZ3lEBp-LakTbTiNHa8PYtFTu2Q5nSMv5wtdAJfcB0kZo2wE7fBKvcgBfaDC8OxY81TVpJKm6cbbO0eVEAxIQ4ina7Uq1UzS11q4dmU2pmqZbYQuk3Vw_oBT8Gf_hQGIWWiz8gkz6i-IiTs-gnWEAvQMGCAKbMNtVC5Iyo98TNyGi8YOdQyNflEyUBbsFqenImIui-2OYB_qZxhvv6AHdsWKxoTJGkJmfooWHBNaw6gW8bQJLlFNAWwk-fs_Si6yUTOSdWvNB6wsvLFonjEDSrzaSabv2_y7tvcPf7ilVxKbT3q04BYHtGhVB2nFPfCN-t2dIQv-3YlxH2BQCLhIJ3H9j30NsNKG76GvIq)
---
|
---
| | FROM THE DESK OF THE PUBLISHER
---
| | Peter, How bad is the New York Times? Well, how better to illustrate than to contrast the Times with The New York Sun? **The Times headline on the left guides the reader towards a justification for the violent terror attack by focusing on the synagogue 's "dedication to a Jewish state." The Sun doesn't play that game--it reports the hard facts, the vehicle used as a weapon, and the fatal shootout with guards.**
---
| | [](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=fa9a72ad36b34d85978c405a0ab77ede&_e=JhVLDxUNwCev6kRqTQYj7-cpxrOIc0WFXiws9Dy-DPLitlolD_EFgMS2cv5-hVQAKW86TOLnBdl_UpKpy9oQ1NXIE0WFYtYUeZRvJHSSaYMUsvSAoNXWkdlS3zomOHvKGbb0zLi-Nqd3qzz42ADXQjme2iChHTRbPGIFJnsRdx0C_4TBmPIqeGcNlxUFAwg-mWnHy9DoBkaqYMYx-KGWOCBDelk2X2XaEp_CDW5ljbTFRVK0vrkTWp8vsoQ8E9xJkknilsN5ExKHZiMcTMn9m4_KgTogbO8lR1Y6T3LXYhrjJ8UDeONbaMH6e1ayYLDkbYLX3jMpdOpTINZ7KR-DqAqGyidzF1xxYnVxKD_I3nbjvUjI57yte5jHhKMIF1bYFQDIGkgS37prPi4xx12gw_5WcwpfBnfLY0govNSpovMWvUS9l87ZmrJpR8z-Vd1irySNkDIbFNF1LszJN6CTFx7qjxKtmkGAYlcDY-SkyVCq97Uj6SNqTkkjTeybr-5CdxaH3ltsx29Fs2sNDuKKULhb20qhkyS834O5_wU4kaDlkJosy9Sr6IadsMfDFj9N)

Well, how better to illustrate than to contrast the Times with The New York Sun? The Times headline on the left guides the reader towards a justification for the violent terror attack by focusing on the synagogue 's "dedication to a Jewish state." As we enter the second quarter of this midterm election year, I am asking for something deeply important. And no, it’s not money.

Today, I am asking for your direct input.

**Will you spare a couple minutes to fill out my campaign’s 2026 Priorities Survey, Derek?** **[TAKE THE SURVEY](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128699575/612092980/-963781499?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_SVY_PrioritiesRS_260403_T1_1&ms=MW_ATA_EM_SVY_PrioritiesRS_260403_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1Y2VjOThjNi02ZDJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QlCdTCJcmOP94Jk6BSa6Zqq6udh2oAvCV7w1p7SQirE=&emci=6533574b-b32e-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&emdi=5cec98c6-6d2f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&ceid=4568237)** So much has happened this year already, I know it feels overwhelming to keep track of the chaos.

Senate Republicans are trying to pass legislation that would strip voting rights from millions of Americans by imposing unnecessary and burdensome verification requirements... The president has dragged us into a war of choice overseas... Nothing is being done by Republicans to address the sky-high cost of living.

But I want to tell you that I’m keeping track of it all, Derek – and I need to know that we’re on the same page as we gear up to fight back in this critical election year.

[_**That’s why I’m asking you to spare two minutes to fill out my campaign’s 2026 Priorities Survey >>**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128699578/612092982/-963781499?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_SVY_PrioritiesRS_260403_T1_1&ms=MW_ATA_EM_SVY_PrioritiesRS_260403_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1Y2VjOThjNi02ZDJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QlCdTCJcmOP94Jk6BSa6Zqq6udh2oAvCV7w1p7SQirE=&emci=6533574b-b32e-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&emdi=5cec98c6-6d2f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&ceid=4568237)
