That executive order... |
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128699726/612093345/-664395954?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTkxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZmVjOThjNi02ZDJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=zH8RC7TIkDDdngqTlEgJCemZ0BwjoVfs0DzYub9npQ0=)
This is bullsh*t, friend. Sorry for swearing, but this is serious: Donald Trump just signed an executive order gutting vote-by-mail and attempting to take over control of our elections. Trump says going forward, his Department of Homeland Security will decide who can vote, and the United States Postal Service will ONLY send ballots to those voters. **This is an unconstitutional, authoritarian overreach designed to STEAL our elections...and I’m not going to let him get away with it.** I’m on the frontlines as the chief elections official of a swing state that Trump is STILL mad that he lost in 2020. They’re trying to replace me this November. But as long as I’m Secretary of State, I’m promising you now: I WON’T let Trump take away your right to vote. **[If you’re with me, split $3 now between DASS and my campaign to help us fight back against Donald Trump’s slimy attempts to steal our elections →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128699732/612093348/-598132470?refcode=em-4.3.26-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTkxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZmVjOThjNi02ZDJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=zH8RC7TIkDDdngqTlEgJCemZ0BwjoVfs0DzYub9npQ0=)** __If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Adrian Fontes and DASS will go through immediately: __ | | | **[Chip in $10 Now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128699740/612093357/-598132470?refcode=em-4.3.26-all-a&express_lane=true&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTkxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZmVjOThjNi02ZDJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=zH8RC7TIkDDdngqTlEgJCemZ0BwjoVfs0DzYub9npQ0=)**

clean_body: That executive order... | = This is bullsht, friend. Sorry for swearing, but this is serious: Donald Trump just signed an executive order gutting vote-by-mail and attempting to take over control of our elections. Trump says going forward, his Department of Homeland Security will decide who can vote, and the United States Postal Service will ONLY send ballots to those voters. This is an unconstitutional, authoritarian overreach designed to STEAL our elections...and I’m not going to let him get away with it. I’m on the frontlines as the chief elections official of a swing state that Trump is STILL mad that he lost in 2020. They’re trying to replace me this November. name: Save the Persecuted Christians
subject: [URGENT] As we rejoice, do not look away:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Save the Persecuted Christians
body:
| | | | [](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=dbc6a64754de4b42b84e580edc51022e&_e=PbcTurQJKDLXwYvx78lHlR2efLEBxa26FNfVBECZcAA1W8i4cB_meW7uIW6olMThOqX89zKg5AhD_VH6822yT5ySL3KjWrstCi_Kl1GkVbLCHGZTXeube8q-NMdQnMQVU3onaA3j0qGGPfUkarKUx36e5K3UO7pVlMsd1GFNKSHc5ZZCcXoHTnkzlUC5aLUjqXWWcl25dd02lDF01npJP5v97Ly9cx8bePUuC0mAU9ZPD9waLavn4EUJuKFYmpYL5gjhRDnzp03nEw1t8DHLxEkg0h6EaGLwfxnssWWbclckSyrsLwMq-4CUpqR2ehAyBqqXFYG0xyHlj9MDcDFPk71Q2NAoDZrQQbx85LrYJAXwkbT5_aFFfxJG0O-OLiokcPM6q23Z_X0ceoStAk8ZdrJOE4JoXPFu4jbz1Dn9UvHslBUQYFHOIx6vjHTYh9XFiwoWRVyVzpRwQM0qJkH1Z-DldwX5Fo1VeLQhaJZeCczjwfkK5xMfLtqZN68kMTl4aAMi_nY8H1j6hsNT5R44DMeS7uxtqrsBWK--_-Ik1kFsWM2r-pQPWkvLYnbdvbhQslXSACM26Whxl78-TdnUz8e8k9wQtRDeHeuVSPVfHm577EwVy-bTk4VXn6Ntd6IHwBMjqsHvTJuOf9mPN5KzsO36BsCpnMuYg7uWVODaSFA%3D)
---
Friend, This Easter, as we rejoice that He is risen, our brothers and sisters in Christ in Nigeria are weeping over fresh graves and tending to the wounded from a brutal new wave of jihadist slaughter. **Just days ago, during Palm Sunday week, terrorists unleashed horror after horror on defenseless Christian communities.** Gunmen stormed neighborhoods, opening fire on families gathered after worship — slaughtering dozens in the streets. **At least 28 believers cut down in one community alone.** That same night, attackers raided a Christian wedding celebration, **mowing down 13 guests and dragging others into the darkness.** Across multiple attacks this week, **nearly 100 precious souls were killed** — fathers, mothers, young believers, entire families wiped out _simply for loving Jesus._ The volume of this evil is staggering. One massacre after another. Blood in the streets. Widows left sobbing. Children orphaned overnight. Survivors maimed for life, crying out in agony while far too many across the world look away. **Easter is one of the most dangerous times of the year for persecuted Christians around the globe.** When the Church gathers visibly to proclaim the risen Savior — singing, worshiping, remembering the cross — the enemy strikes with fury. Year after year, hundreds die in Easter attacks. **This Palm Sunday massacre is a heartbreaking reminder that for millions, Holy Week means terror, not triumph.** Yet amid the bloodshed, they still refuse to deny Christ. Their unshakeable faith in the empty tomb humbles and convicts us. **Here at Save the Persecuted Christians, the cries of these suffering saints demand our immediate response. We ended Q1 short of our goal, and the needs are exploding — more widows, more orphans, more displaced families facing starvation and fear.**
---
[SAVE CHRISTIANS](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=dbc6a64754de4b42b84e580edc51022e&_e=PbcTurQJKDLXwYvx78lHlR2efLEBxa26FNfVBECZcAA1W8i4cB_meW7uIW6olMThOqX89zKg5AhD_VH6822yT5ySL3KjWrstCi_Kl1GkVbLCHGZTXeube8q-NMdQnMQVU3onaA3j0qGGPfUkarKUx36e5K3UO7pVlMsd1GFNKSHc5ZZCcXoHTnkzlUC5aLUjqXWWcl25dd02lDF01npJP5v97Ly9cx8bePUuC0mAU9Z7W4oXxOAD-_YhBfzAlFBH2jLbVxlGpCTGYffcjmaX7RVat7bUaSOXsKlT_THs--k1niLpc_NkQwIWeIPrNZG7m31jXgCWViLkzaUFSinRIIBOFo-8nGiXS15a5JvUmjbZb9fw4zFGKOi6_ZUntKmGOVItl1dE74hWLE6MlRFFX3GMwkb7Ze2sr5fzUqEc1eG2VZP-caVUKo7xXHg4ukZNPHr2dR19L0GOS-hqwhGIy9FiVsQeLlWvruLv1OloLnLdm2iq3KA9xVyApQellOjRUf85WnGc1ritRmC27AmhPP59COsysmeJ1U26BjluJboqS-WpVXyRgu4n9RjqM-pNj0gKEWit5OctzEixTuETfR6asbn-xokMs0HUAF7ItcHQsNf4qNLjgJKcEsP4dkVFJurjFyie486iwz7lyaw-Phi_JOw2nox2busEdlDet6I%3D)
---
These precious believers desperately need our prayers. But beyond that they need a loud, persistent voice raised on their behalf — exposing the evil to the world, rallying the global Church to stand with them, and pressing leaders in D.C. and abroad to take real action that stops the slaughter and protects the vulnerable.

**[Your gift today will fuel our advocacy, amplify their stories, open doors with policymakers, and send a powerful message to the persecuted: You are seen. You are not alone. The risen Christ stands with you — and so do we.](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=dbc6a64754de4b42b84e580edc51022e&_e=PbcTurQJKDLXwYvx78lHlR2efLEBxa26FNfVBECZcAA1W8i4cB_meW7uIW6olMThOqX89zKg5AhD_VH6822yT5ySL3KjWrstCi_Kl1GkVbLCHGZTXeube8q-NMdQnMQVU3onaA3j0qGGPfUkarKUx36e5K3UO7pVlMsd1GFNKSHc5ZZCcXoHTnkzlUC5aLUjqXWWcl25dd02lDF01npJP5v97Ly9cx8bePUuC0mAU9Zh7zeTBH2mYPqiTFNmGBJ3f83oHMm_seQ9YY4tu4XbBlMCGB8Y5740CX2SHifjGsWdN2kp2Hwr37dh5hqzoEcnm_N4d6afep-un6SllObkfyfQrKz7lEyOCKX9fA5b_p-JLe_fJpVcZwuRGLLnQWEZgcRzg0-xV9gMk-IyKeFNH6qkbTvidirI8NBgm2vOIShF5GFRgWVmaS7zJ9aHs6_14NT7P8KP0nxjcG-SXJ0DDko-deNcw_WdUoqu60EAKHm1fFeLYmM9oE6AhcLSSjng6n34Wi2QFy4EpA1slTBekgrgsad_NokwJdCy5ZzNzDZBNeR0BvlOKJtLP2cW9I_PDDt0h6XYg7U9ylM_VJuiBb1iFnQA_0ySsB1V0w-U24vEQBTo6OQX4slq63yy1WpVI9KheYPu54MmXtGvC0x2zAGJC9ifJVRL596OVjiVnsw%3D)**

_Will you act now?_ Every hour we hesitate, more blood may be shed. More families shattered. Your generosity right now declares: The risen Christ has overcome the grave — and we will not abandon His suffering Church.

He is risen. Let’s act like it — urgently, sacrificially, with hearts fully engaged.

In faith and urgent prayer, Dede Laugesen
President & CEO
Save the Persecuted Christians

name: Breaking:
subject: Voter turnout collapses in major race for Dems
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY DEFEND OUR COURTS, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE
body:
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0017wXRr_SgGJr8KWAsYVH26Qzu6ZilWGQeJewtu1FHB8FmFXKoZucj0ysnhO9qGOdQ9y5nCwUc5GikqPNmBPxNNsnhPkXF9uZWDXGlZmyCp0xNxjRqd7_E08CwFiwlP9Tq_nD1La4H81LM8-C4-JzZLyV0jKDt0uSIMaUC6OjPpq47GYjvEd-XfBxH5IhVwoQMDijO0D7Kh8C-qnU-JwsbTS5XRLbBt4z1erVt5bK5nOY=&c=8_nAd0MRH51W6KLxdIyU8tjTiyw5DTzEhJZMJWKzIc48LZNl407HTA==&ch=xaMEK0ZBe2y6d-kzqNpmK7UPW5JI3HhAp60J-zcxy7Mc_TdXBaqbAQ==)
---
|
Peter,
Last April, records were shattered in the Wisconsin Supreme Court election.
Two million voters showed up. Over $100 million spent. Elon Musk flew to Wisconsin and handed out million-dollar checks. Democrats won anyway.
But this year's April 7th WI Supreme Court race is a completely different story and it could be catastrophic for Democrats.
Absentee ballots are down 112,000 from this point last year. Spending is down 90%. A new poll shows only 12% of Wisconsin voters have heard anything about this race.

---
| [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0017wXRr_SgGJr8KWAsYVH26Qzu6ZilWGQeJewtu1FHB8FmFXKoZucj0ysnhO9qGOdQ9y5nCwUc5GikqPNmBPxNNsnhPkXF9uZWDXGlZmyCp0xNxjRqd7_E08CwFiwlP9Tq_nD1La4H81LM8-C4-JzZLyV0jKDt0uSIMaUC6OjPpq47GYjvEd-XfBxH5IhVwoQMDijO0D7Kh8C-qnU-JwsbTbesehx11h85yn34csA-Qso=&c=8_nAd0MRH51W6KLxdIyU8tjTiyw5DTzEhJZMJWKzIc48LZNl407HTA==&ch=xaMEK0ZBe2y6d-kzqNpmK7UPW5JI3HhAp60J-zcxy7Mc_TdXBaqbAQ==)
---

This is how MAGA wins.
And a loss here would be devastating for Dems--it would put MAGA on a path to flip the WI Supreme Court just months before the 2028 presidential election.
State supreme courts decide which votes count, how districts get drawn, and whether any challenge to an election outcome gets heard. Put MAGA judges on that bench and every one of those decisions gets made in MAGA's favor in 2028.
Right now, Defend Our Courts has identified 588,000 Democratic voters in Wisconsin and we're desperately scrambling to turnout as many of them as possible in these final 4 days.
But we don't have the resources to reach all of them. Not yet.
Every dollar we raise in the next 4 days lets us contact more voters -- voters who are largely undecided, in a race where a few thousand votes could decide everything.
Our efforts could be the difference in this race. That's why we're asking you today:
[Peter, there are only 4 days until election day and it's coming down to a mad dash to turnout as many voters as possible. Can we count on you to rush a donation of $50, $25, or whatever you can spare to help us turnout as many Democrats as we possibly can in these final days and defend the WI Supreme Court?](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0017wXRr_SgGJr8KWAsYVH26Qzu6ZilWGQeJewtu1FHB8FmFXKoZucj0ysnhO9qGOdQ-X-xGYtpJLJBMPCXYQGyHAnIz5BXYGvELvKnIj8fW2VUpNzxW6HTVWYBPWSyGjVu4GSC4JQrNkii9fyUptoG8rtJOK9WLt_EsbRdPBVur6vJ0oG_bjr_GzijTtqGG2gOsUDqbv_MbetBzgdBgj6OT1PYia1dK7B8xx07TINV60_9P4sxWwbYk6fB4-d6bH4A&c=8_nAd0MRH51W6KLxdIyU8tjTiyw5DTzEhJZMJWKzIc48LZNl407HTA==&ch=xaMEK0ZBe2y6d-kzqNpmK7UPW5JI3HhAp60J-zcxy7Mc_TdXBaqbAQ==)

clean_body: | | | | | == | Peter, Last April, records were shattered in the Wisconsin Supreme Court election. Two million voters showed up. Over $100 million spent. Elon Musk flew to Wisconsin and handed out million-dollar checks. Democrats won anyway. But this year's April 7th WI Supreme Court race is a completely different story and it could be catastrophic for Democrats. Absentee ballots are down 112,000 from this point last year. Spending is down 90%. A new poll shows only 12% of Wisconsin voters have heard anything about this race. | == This is how MAGA wins. And a loss here would be devastating for Dems--it would put MAGA on a path to flip the WI Supreme Court just months before the 2028 presidential election. State supreme courts decide which votes count, how districts get drawn, and whether any challenge to an election outcome gets heard. Put MAGA judges on that bench and every one of those decisions gets made in MAGA's favor in 2028. Right now, Defend Our Courts has identified 588,000 Democratic voters in Wisconsin and we're desperately scrambling to turnout as many of them as possible in these final 4 days. But we don't have the resources to reach all of them. Not yet. Every dollar we raise in the next 4 days lets us contact more voters -- voters who are largely undecided, in a race where a few thousand votes could decide everything. Our efforts could be the difference in this race. That's why we're asking you today: == | | | == | == | == | | | == | == | == | | | | == | Only 4 days. 588,000 Democrats. We need your help to reach all of them. name: Ruben Gallego
subject: Trump is attacking Arizona’s elections
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Gallego for Arizona
body:
Donald Trump just signed an executive order to restrict mail-in voting. He wants to tell us who can and can’t vote.
#

Unbelievable, Peter.
**Donald Trump just signed an executive order to restrict mail-in voting. He wants to tell us who can and can’t vote.**
[](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-hobbs-em-split.OtSY65&refcode=em260403-1-90&t=1&akid=4110%2E734936%2El4IT0L)
Trump has no constitutional role in our elections. None. It’s already illegal for non-citizens to vote in Arizona, and it’s going to stay that way. This isn’t about that — it’s Trump trying to purge legal voters in key states like ours. It’s a blatant effort at rigging our elections, and we can’t let it stand.
_But what can we do about it?_
[**For my part, I’m backing Arizona’s Governor, Katie Hobbs. She’s fighting back against Trump with everything she’s got. If you have her back, split a donation between her campaign and mine to support her re-election and keep her on the frontlines defending our voting rights →**](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-hobbs-em-split.OtSY65&refcode=em260403-1-90&t=2&akid=4110%2E734936%2El4IT0L)

Friend, I'm not going to ask you for money today. I just want to be honest about where we are. We spent the last few months saying things most people in Washington won't. That ICE needs to be abolished and its agents prosecuted, that a draft dodger has no business floating a military draft for a war with no plan or strategy, and that Democrats need to have an actual plan for what comes after Trump. I published an **[op-ed](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128702552/612101243/-177738211?refcode=260403_sm_eoqty&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMTNhNThkYS03MDJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=S1xVWLoIeF-oDMt3TPGqoW4Z2G148zSrHdq47wwLJ2o=&emci=014321a9-ad2e-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&emdi=e13a58da-702f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&ceid=194769)** showing the similarities between what's happening with Iran and the lies that sent my fellow Marines and me to Iraq. But here's what I keep coming back to: the people who showed up at events across the commonwealth this quarter aren't looking for the status quo, and recognize that leadership that was first elected 50 years ago isn’t ready to fight the 21st century’s most pressing battles. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128702553/612101244/2090747700?refcode=260403_sm_eoqty_img&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMTNhNThkYS03MDJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=S1xVWLoIeF-oDMt3TPGqoW4Z2G148zSrHdq47wwLJ2o=) In Salem, in Quincy, in Andover, and across Western Mass and the South Coast, people turned out, brought their neighbors, engaged, and asked hard questions. We had delegates show up at caucuses across the commonwealth to put us on the ballot, and rooms that just kept getting fuller. The momentum is real, and I’m grateful to have you on the team. This quarter's going to be bigger and harder. I'm ready for it, and I'm glad to have you with me. Thank you. — Seth P.S. If you want to stay close to this between emails — the unfiltered stuff, clips from the trail, the things I'm probably not supposed to say on TV — come find us. name: New PA Project Education Fund
subject: Don’t Hit Delete: This Could Affect Your Freedom to Vote
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New PA Project Education Fund
body:
<a clicktracking=off href="http://example.com">
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/NPP_Ed_logo_RGB_green1-01.jpg]
Hi Friend,
Welcome to the latest New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) newsletter!
Earlier this week, the PA [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] House State Government Committee [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] voted on two bills that have the potential to impact our voting rights: State Rep Mehaffie's Voter ID bill (HB771) and State Rep. McClinton's omnibus bill (HB1396). Both passed out of committee this week and will now be voted on by the full house.
An omnibus bill is a legislative bill that combines multiple, sometimes unrelated, provisions or bills into one single bill. It's essentially a package deal of different legislative proposals, often used to facilitate the passage of legislation, particularly appropriations bills.
HB 771 is sponsored by State Rep. Thomas Mehaffie (Dauphin) and “would require all voters arriving at their election precincts to show identification or attest under penalty of law that they are legally able to vote in Pennsylvania.”
State Rep. Malcolm Kenyatta (Philadelphia) voiced his opposition to the bill. The bill doesn’t address [https://www.politicspa.com/state-government-committee-sends-voter-id-election-omnibus-bills-to-house-floor/141399] the real issues at stake. “We need to solve problems of voter irregularities and voter fraud,” he said. “We do not have demonstrable evidence that leads us to make the point that we need to address voter ID in this way. Let’s talk about the issues that do exist. This legislation doesn’t address those.”
HB 1396 has a more recognizable name behind it – Speaker of the State House Joanna McClinton (Delaware/Philadelphia). State Rep. Ben Waxman (Philadelphia) complemented the Speaker, saying that the legislation is a “comprehensive approach to election reform that tries to dramatically improve the process and makes administration of elections much smoother.”
Among other changes, [https://penncapital-star.com/campaigns-elections/voter-id-and-vote-by-mail-reform-bills-move-to-pa-house] House Bill 1396 would also give election workers up to a week before Election Day to prepare to count mail-in ballots, a process that has been a bottleneck for election results in parts of the state, providing fodder for election deniers.
The vote-by-mail provision has been the subject of numerous lawsuits, including one now before the U.S. Supreme Court, as candidates and parties have argued over how election officials should handle irregularities, such as errors on completed ballots, that are not explicitly addressed.
The bill would make clear that county election officials are required to notify voters if their mail-in ballots have been rejected for the lack of a signature and give the voter an opportunity to “cure” the error.
Upcoming events
As Allegheny County voters prepare for the pivotal 2025 local, municipal and judicial elections, the New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) is hosting two critical public candidate forums to give community members the chance to hear directly from the candidates shaping our schools and courts. These moderated discussions will allow voters to ask questions and learn more about each candidate’s priorities and qualifications before heading to the polls.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/Pittsburgh%20School%20board%20forum%208.5x11.png]
Meet the candidates running for the Pittsburgh School Board and hear how they plan to impact our schools and students.
Moderator: Dr. Kimeka Campbell is our Senior Director of Campaigns and Engagement. She is a recognized community activist, political strategist, and civic engagement leader.
With over 15 years of experience in community organizing, political consulting, and nonprofit leadership, Dr. Campbell has been instrumental in shaping voter engagement strategies and building coalitions for progressive change. She co-founded Young Professionals of Color–Greater Harrisburg (YPOC), an organization dedicated to fostering leadership, advocacy, and professional growth for communities of color.
When: Friday, May 9, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 7:30 PM
Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221
RSVP [https://click.everyaction.com/k/106793662/542679746/-435660973]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/Allegheny%20judicial%20flyer.png]
Get to know the Allegheny County Common Pleas judicial candidates before you cast your ballot.
Your vote shapes our justice system!
Moderator: Rachel Bracken, Alliance for Justice (AFJ), Senior Regional State Courts Manager
Rachel is a Senior Regional State Courts Manager with Alliance for Justice/AFJ Action. In this position, she manages AFJ/AFJ Action’s involvement in state supreme court justice elections and selection processes. Prior to joining the Justice Team, Rachel was on AFJ’s Outreach Team where she helped create AFJ’s State Court Justice Project and launch AFJ Action’s State Courts Matter initiative. She also coordinated AFJ’s networks of law professors, attorneys, and political donors to advocate around federal judicial nominations and federal courts’ impact on our constitutional rights and freedoms.
When: Monday, May 12, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 8:00 PM
Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221
RSVP [https://click.everyaction.com/k/106793661/542679745/229023784]
Social Media of the Week
https://www.instagram.com/p/DJbyV6DPLdJ/ [https://www.instagram.com/p/DJbyV6DPLdJ/]
The deadline to request your mail-in ballot for the primary election is May 13th at 5PM.
Request a mail-in ballot or find your local ballot drop-off location here [vote.pa]
Your county election office must receive your completed mail ballot by 8PM on May 20th.
We recommend mailing it at least 5 days prior, or delivering it by hand at an official ballot drop off location.
#GoVotePA
SHARE ON INSTAGRAM [https://www.instagram.com/p/DJbyV6DPLdJ/]
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/7237344C447445787878394747342B75584E47375A57552F745265427662334B544A4A36505A58657A5871474B7A5A6E53705571424758325657365969365747/editor_images/WCM%20logo.JPG] #CourtsMatter
This past Friday, Courts delivered wins for Pennsylvania voters [https://www.democracydocket.com/news-alerts/a-double-win-for-voters-in-pennsylvania] . One ruling ensured that undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots will be counted, while the other rejected a bid to purge more than 277,000 from the state’s voter rolls.
First, the 3rd U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals denied [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/445-2025-04-24-Order-denying-PA-Attorney-Generals-motion-to-intervene.pdf] a request by Pennsylvania Attorney General Dave Sunday (R), on behalf of the state, to halt a recent federal court ruling that Pennsylvania must count undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots.
The ruling stemmed from a lawsuit originally filed [https://www.democracydocket.com/cases/pennsylvania-undated-and-wrongly-dated-mail-in-ballots-challenge-eakin/] in 2022, challenging the state’s instructions to county boards of elections that any mail-in ballot that’s timely cast but either missing a date or containing the wrong date on the outer return envelopes should not be counted.
After the federal judge’s ruling in March of this year, the plaintiffs appealed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/441-2025-04-02-Notice-Of-Appeal-.pdf] the decision to the 3rd Circuit. The appeal remains ongoing, but today’s order ensures that counties will count wrongly dated and undated ballots during this month’s primary.
Second, a Pennsylvania district court judge dismissed a lawsuit by the 1789 Foundation to have hundreds of thousands of voters purged from Pennsylvania’s voter rolls.The 1789 Foundation, also known as Citizen_AG, filed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/1-2024-10-29-Complaint.pdf] a lawsuit in October against Pennsylvania Secretary of State Al Schmidt (R) claiming Schmidt failed to maintain voter rolls in line with the National Voter Registration Act.
The group wanted the court to force Pennsylvania to remove 277,000 registrants who did not respond to confirmation notices in the 2020 general election cycle removed from the voter rolls.
District Court judge Robert Mariani dismissed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/67-2025-05-02-Memorandum-.pdf] the 1789 Foundation’s lawsuit on the grounds that the organization lacked standing to bring the case in the first place.
In law, standing [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Standing_(law)] is a condition that a party seeking a legal remedy must show they have, by demonstrating to the court, sufficient connection to and harm from the law or action challenged to support the party's participation in the case.
In other news, this year marks the 15th anniversary of a landmark U.S. Supreme Court ruling that fundamentally reshaped the world of campaign finance. For good-government supporters, it’s a somber milestone. Find out why in Spotlight PA’s full report. [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/citizens-united-supreme-court-ruling-campaign-finance-dark-money]
The 2010 decision in Citizens United v. Federal Election Commission , they say, vastly expanded the role of big money in politics and ushered in an era of unprecedented — and in some cases, untraceable — spending on elections by ultra-wealthy special interests.
In Pennsylvania, the court decision’s impact on races for state and local offices was muted — at least early on. That is because of Pennsylvania’s notoriously lax campaign finance laws. Some state lawmakers have sought to tighten rules around so-called “dark money” groups, but their legislation didn’t make it through the full legislature. Continue reading for more on Citizens United and its lasting impact [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/citizens-united-supreme-court-ruling-campaign-finance-dark-money] .
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/DelCo_2.png] We are hiring!
Let's provide civic education together!
We're looking for people passionate about voting rights in Philadelphia, Allegheny, Erie, Lawrence, Bucks, Montgomery, Chester, and Delaware County to help us shape the future of Pennsylvania.
Join our team and help us provide civic education in our communities and register folks to vote!
Positions start at $20 an hour.
Interested? Contact jobs@nppef.org [jobs@nppef.org]
EMAIL US [jobs@nppef.org]
News You Can Use
There are things every one of us can do [https://www.democracydocket.com/opinion/we-have-more-power-than-trump-wants-us-to-believe-heres-what-you-can-do/] in our daily lives to help ensure free and fair elections. Some actions are small — so small that you might dismiss them as unimportant. Don’t. Every important journey begins with a single step, and the first is no less important than the last.
1. Educate Yourself
Elections can be complicated. The rules vary from state to state — and sometimes even from county to county. These rules also change frequently due to new laws, policies or court rulings. My first recommendation: spend time each month learning what’s happening with voting laws in your state and nationally.
Spotlight PA has an 2025 Election Center [https://www.spotlightpa.org/elections] with all the information you need to be prepared to vote this year, including guides to the big races, key dates, and more.
* New: Which Pittsburgh mayoral candidate most aligns with you? Take our quiz [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pittsburgh-mayor-election-2025-candidates-quiz]
* Get answers to your questions with our AI-powered Election Assistant [https://www.spotlightpa.org/elections/assistant/]
* Everything you need to know about mail voting [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-mail-ballot-how-to-request-fill-out-return] , becoming a poll worker [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-how-to-be-poll-worker-guide]
* Everything you need to know about how to vote and where to vote [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pennsylvania-primary-election-day-2025-voter-information-registration]
* Guides to Commonwealth, Superior Courts [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-commonwealth-superior-court-candidates] , vetting judicial candidates [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-candidate-vetting-guide]
* Guides to State College school board [https://www.spotlightpa.org/statecollege/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-state-college-school-board-anderson-huff-barlow-black-desmarais-mcternan-sogor] , Reading City Council president [https://www.spotlightpa.org/berks/2025/04/reading-city-council-president-primary-election-2025]
* Guides to voting as a college student [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-college-student-voting-guide]
En Español
* Guía para votar por correo [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pensilvania-elecciones-primarias-2025-votar-correo-como-llenar] , la presidencia del Concejo Municipal de Reading [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/reading-concejo-municipal-primarias-elecciones-2025]
* Guía para quién está en la papeleta de las primarias [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pensilvania-primarias-elecciones-2025-votar-informacion] , cómo convertirse en trabajador electoral el 20 de mayo [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pensilvania-elecciones-2025-como-convertirse-trabajador-electoral]
2. Stay Engaged
To protect democracy, we all must stay engaged day in and day out. We can’t just show up right before an election and tune out afterward. If we remain committed, democracy will win.
Check out a Montgomery County Commissioner’s effort to educate voters. It's now an emmy-nominated short film. “Running elections in Pennsylvania's third-most-populous county, Neil Makhija said he’s seen the slippery slope from election misinformation to political violence.” ( Philadelphia Inquirer [https://www.inquirer.com/politics/pennsylvania/montgomery-county-commissioner-neil-makhija-emmy-20250508.html] )
3. Share What You Learn
Once you’ve figured out what’s going on, share that information with your networks and community. It might feel awkward to bring up voter suppression or changes in election laws when we’re not in the middle of a major election — but local elections happen in off years, and special elections are more common than you think. Now is the perfect time to start conversations about voting.
Here, in Pennsylvania, we vote every year, twice a year!
4. Join or Support Pro-Democracy Organizations
Is partisan politics not for you? There are numerous nonprofits and grassroots organizations working every day to protect voting rights and ensure free and fair elections. Supporting these groups with your time or resources helps these groups do their work.
Check out the work New PA Project has been up to lately below. AND , check out our upcoming events listed above!
5. Become a Trained Poll Worker
Many counties face a shortage of poll workers, especially during election season. Being a poll worker requires dedication, attention to detail and a willingness to be trained. It’s a meaningful way to serve your community and help protect the integrity of the voting process.
Here are some resources that may be helpful:
Pennsylvania Capital-Star - Advocacy groups offer training for Pa. primary poll watchers [https://penncapital-star.com/briefs/advocacy-groups-offer-training-for-pa-primary-poll-watchers/]
Spotlight PA - Election 2025: How to become a poll worker for the PA primary [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-how-to-be-poll-worker-guide/]
Franklin County Free Press - Poll Workers Needed for Pennsylvania’s 2025 Primary Election [https://fcfreepresspa.com/poll-workers-needed-for-pennsylvanias-2025-primary-election/]
6. Engage Your Elected Officials
Call and write your elected officials at the federal, state and local levels. Tell them that protecting voting rights and ensuring free and fair elections are your top priorities. Even better, show up at public meetings. Ask them directly what they’re doing to combat voter suppression. Be informed about pending legislation and urge them to support pro-democracy initiatives. This applies no matter where you live or who represents you — Democrat or Republican. Constituent pressure always matters. Don’t let anyone tell you otherwise.
7. Vote in Every Election
Yes — every election. That means local elections, primary elections and special elections. Become a super-voter. The more you vote, the more informed and engaged you become. You’ll also be better positioned to notice and report problems — like changes to polling locations or voting equipment — that could impact turnout or accessibility.
Make a plan to vote in the primary election, Tuesday, May 20th. When are you voting this year? Who are you going with? How are you voting this year -- by mail or on Election Day? As a reminder, the last day to request a mail ballot is Tuesday, May 13th!

clean_body: [] Hi Friend, Welcome to the latest New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) newsletter! Earlier this week, the PA [] House State Government Committee [] [] voted on two bills that have the potential to impact our voting rights: State Rep Mehaffie's Voter ID bill (HB771) and State Rep. McClinton's omnibus bill (HB1396). Both passed out of committee this week and will now be voted on by the full house. An omnibus bill is a legislative bill that combines multiple, sometimes unrelated, provisions or bills into one single bill. It's essentially a package deal of different legislative proposals, often used to facilitate the passage of legislation, particularly appropriations bills. HB 771 is sponsored by State Rep. Thomas Mehaffie (Dauphin) and “would require all voters arriving at their election precincts to show identification or attest under penalty of law that they are legally able to vote in Pennsylvania.” State Rep. Malcolm Kenyatta (Philadelphia) voiced his opposition to the bill. The bill doesn’t address [] the real issues at stake. “We need to solve problems of voter irregularities and voter fraud,” he said. “We do not have demonstrable evidence that leads us to make the point that we need to address voter ID in this way. Let’s talk about the issues that do exist. This legislation doesn’t address those.” HB 1396 has a more recognizable name behind it – Speaker of the State House Joanna McClinton (Delaware/Philadelphia). State Rep. Ben Waxman (Philadelphia) complemented the Speaker, saying that the legislation is a “comprehensive approach to election reform that tries to dramatically improve the process and makes administration of elections much smoother.” Among other changes, [] House Bill 1396 would also give election workers up to a week before Election Day to prepare to count mail-in ballots, a process that has been a bottleneck for election results in parts of the state, providing fodder for election deniers. The vote-by-mail provision has been the subject of numerous lawsuits, including one now before the U.S. Supreme Court, as candidates and parties have argued over how election officials should handle irregularities, such as errors on completed ballots, that are not explicitly addressed. The bill would make clear that county election officials are required to notify voters if their mail-in ballots have been rejected for the lack of a signature and give the voter an opportunity to “cure” the error. Upcoming events As Allegheny County voters prepare for the pivotal 2025 local, municipal and judicial elections, the New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) is hosting two critical public candidate forums to give community members the chance to hear directly from the candidates shaping our schools and courts. These moderated discussions will allow voters to ask questions and learn more about each candidate’s priorities and qualifications before heading to the polls. [] Meet the candidates running for the Pittsburgh School Board and hear how they plan to impact our schools and students. Moderator: Dr. Kimeka Campbell is our Senior Director of Campaigns and Engagement. She is a recognized community activist, political strategist, and civic engagement leader. With over 15 years of experience in community organizing, political consulting, and nonprofit leadership, Dr. Campbell has been instrumental in shaping voter engagement strategies and building coalitions for progressive change. She co-founded Young Professionals of Color–Greater Harrisburg (YPOC), an organization dedicated to fostering leadership, advocacy, and professional growth for communities of color. When: Friday, May 9, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 7:30 PM Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221 RSVP [] [] Get to know the Allegheny County Common Pleas judicial candidates before you cast your ballot. Your vote shapes our justice system! Moderator: Rachel Bracken, Alliance for Justice (AFJ), Senior Regional State Courts Manager Rachel is a Senior Regional State Courts Manager with Alliance for Justice/AFJ Action. In this position, she manages AFJ/AFJ Action’s involvement in state supreme court justice elections and selection processes. Prior to joining the Justice Team, Rachel was on AFJ’s Outreach Team where she helped create AFJ’s State Court Justice Project and launch AFJ Action’s State Courts Matter initiative. She also coordinated AFJ’s networks of law professors, attorneys, and political donors to advocate around federal judicial nominations and federal courts’ impact on our constitutional rights and freedoms. There are numerous nonprofits and grassroots organizations working every day to protect voting rights and ensure free and fair elections. Supporting these groups with your time or resources helps these groups do their work. Check out the work New PA Project has been up to lately below. AND , check out our upcoming events listed above! 5. Become a Trained Poll Worker Many counties face a shortage of poll workers, especially during election season. Being a poll worker requires dedication, attention to detail and a willingness to be trained. It’s a meaningful way to serve your community and help protect the integrity of the voting process. Here are some resources that may be helpful: Pennsylvania Capital-Star - Advocacy groups offer training for Pa. primary poll watchers [] Spotlight PA - Election 2025: How to become a poll worker for the PA primary [] Franklin County Free Press - Poll Workers Needed for Pennsylvania’s 2025 Primary Election [] 6. Engage Your Elected Officials Call and write your elected officials at the federal, state and local levels. Tell them that protecting voting rights and ensuring free and fair elections are your top priorities. Even better, show up at public meetings. Ask them directly what they’re doing to combat voter suppression. Be informed about pending legislation and urge them to support pro-democracy initiatives. This applies no matter where you live or who represents you — Democrat or Republican. Constituent pressure always matters. Don’t let anyone tell you otherwise. 7. Vote in Every Election Yes — every election. That means local elections, primary elections and special elections. Become a super-voter. The more you vote, the more informed and engaged you become. You’ll also be better positioned to notice and report problems — like changes to polling locations or voting equipment — that could impact turnout or accessibility. Make a plan to vote in the primary election, Tuesday, May 20th. When are you voting this year? Who are you going with? How are you voting this year -- by mail or on Election Day? As a reminder, the last day to request a mail ballot is Tuesday, May 13th! Recent Events [] Solidarity for Immigrants On Monday, May 5th, also Cinco de Mayo, over 300 people, representing 18 community organizations from across Pennsylvania, gathered at the State Capitol in Harrisburg to rally for immigrant rights. We amplified voices that have been silenced, advocated against injustice, and showed that solidarity is not just a word—it is a force that creates change. This event was a declaration that Pennsylvania stands against injustice and division. [] [] [] NPPEF on the Ground [] Last Monday, May 5th was the last day to register to vote for the primary election! Our teams were out in their communities leading up to the voter registration deadline on Monday. We helped folks ensure they have the ability to exercise their right to vote in the primary on Tuesday, May 20th. Our teams are currently getting ready for GOTV - a time of year where we are on the ground connecting with our communities and mobilizing them to vote. It's our favorite time of year to build relationships with those we hope to uplift. Dauphin County [] [] Delaware County [] [] Luzerne County [] [] Super voters vote twice a year, every year ! Thank you for doing your part to stay informed and engaged! [] Last day to register to request a mail ballot: Tuesday, May 13th Primary Election: Tuesday, May 20th Last day to register to vote for the General Election: Monday, October 20th Last day to request mail-in ballot: Tuesday, October 28th General Election: Tuesday, November 4th Important Links: Voter Hotlines: PA Department of State Website [vote.pa.gov] Voter Protection Hotline: 1-866-OUR-VOTE Upcoming elections [] DOS PA Voter Hotline: 1-877-VOTESPA Mail-in and Absentee Ballot [] Spanish/English Hotline: 1-866-VE-Y-VOTA Fact-Checking Election Claims [] Asian Languages Hotline: 1-888-API-VOTE Arabic/English Hotline: 1-844-YALLA-US Paid for by the New PA Project Education Fund New PA Project Education Fund PO Box 453 West Chester, PA 19381 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

Peter,
On Wednesday, our legal team stood in front of the Colorado title board and fought for our best shot at countering MAGA's election rigging scheme. The title board has final say over ballot measure language and the vote threshold required to pass.
Trump sent his own lawyer to personally argue against our ballot initiative.
The title board sided with Trump's lawyer, and shot down our best proposal.
The board left us two paths forward. Two separate initiatives, each needing a simple majority. Or one combined measure where we'd need to clear 55%. A threshold MAGA is banking on us never hitting.
Both paths are unacceptable.

body:
No one likes a copycat. |

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128704069/612104094/-1080350292?refcode=WUEFEP05_20260403_MagaFlop_ALL_v1s4&sourceid=1058251&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNzBmYWIyZC03MjJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=z7EQUYaB_5Wz2DsEqKZJ62Tz2V0zMHHk8r50OgDSHw0=) Have you heard, Peter?

The NRCC is launching a new program aimed at expanding the Republican U.S. House majority — coined "MAGA Majority." Sounds a lot like our Mission Majority initiative, launched back in February, doesn't it?

We'll take it as a compliment. [_**Donate today to support our Mission Majority and help make sure Republicans' copycat program never gets off the ground.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128704072/612104097/-1080350292?refcode=WUEFEP05_20260403_MagaFlop_ALL_v1s4&sourceid=1058251&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNzBmYWIyZC03MjJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=z7EQUYaB_5Wz2DsEqKZJ62Tz2V0zMHHk8r50OgDSHw0=) | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128704075/612104103/-1282862128?refcode=WUEFEP05_20260403_MagaFlop_ALL_v1s4&sourceid=1058251&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNzBmYWIyZC03MjJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=z7EQUYaB_5Wz2DsEqKZJ62Tz2V0zMHHk8r50OgDSHw0=)

Mission Majority is how we're building the battlefield to take back the House in 2026. And it's no surprise our strategy has inspired copycats — we're recruiting, training, and working alongside the strongest candidates in the most critical races, and we have over 40 years of experience showing how good we are at what we do.

And that's the difference between us and them: We have the candidates. We have the infrastructure. We have the track record. And we have _you_.

The NRCC can launch all the programs they want. Our job — and yours — is to make sure Mission Majority is so strong, so well-funded, and so ready to win that Republicans' majority doesn't just fail to grow — it collapses.

We're not just playing defense. We're going on the offensive — targeting the seats we need to flip, supporting the candidates who can win them, and making sure every dollar Republicans have invested in their new program is wasted.
[_**Can you make a contribution to support our Mission Majority initiative today? Every dollar goes toward electing Democratic pro-choice women and taking back the House this November.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128704079/612104113/-1080350292?refcode=WUEFEP05_20260403_MagaFlop_ALL_v1s4&sourceid=1058251&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNzBmYWIyZC03MjJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=z7EQUYaB_5Wz2DsEqKZJ62Tz2V0zMHHk8r50OgDSHw0=)

Sounds a lot like our Mission Majority initiative, launched back in February, doesn't it? We'll take it as a compliment. = | = Mission Majority is how we're building the battlefield to take back the House in 2026. And it's no surprise our strategy has inspired copycats — we're recruiting, training, and working alongside the strongest candidates in the most critical races, and we have over 40 years of experience showing how good we are at what we do. And that's the difference between us and them: We have the candidates. We have the infrastructure. We have the track record. And we have you. The NRCC can launch all the programs they want. Our job — and yours — is to make sure Mission Majority is so strong, so well-funded, and so ready to win that Republicans' majority doesn't just fail to grow — it collapses. We're not just playing defense. We're going on the offensive — targeting the seats we need to flip, supporting the candidates who can win them, and making sure every dollar Republicans have invested in their new program is wasted. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Talk soon, Amanda Sherman Baity (she/her/hers) Chief Communications Officer, EMILYs List | = | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. name: Team Marsha Blackburn
subject: Peter, you’re an all-star!
body:
You’ve blown us away with your dedication!

| | | | Peter! You’re the MVP! You’re the all-star!
We’ve recently reviewed your record and, WOW, you blew us away.
For years, you’ve gone above and beyond to support conservative efforts! We want to reward you for your exceptional support!
[Which is why we are offering you the exclusive opportunity to claim your Gold VIP status!](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn.-TLjAQEXKfd9MDN_3R-OUQ)[](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn.) | [ CLAIM YOUR GOLD VIP MEMBERSHIP ](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn.)
---
We don’t see patriots like you very often, so we wanted you to know how much we appreciate you.

Without people like you, our conservative movement wouldn’t be possible. So once again, and from the bottom of our hearts: THANK YOU!
[ CLAIM YOUR GOLD VIP MEMBERSHIP ](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn.-l6bXdDtCHjUw)
---
We look forward to continuing our valuable work together!

\- Team Marsha Blackburn

As an instructor in Georgia State’s nursing school, I’ve spent years teaching and supporting the students who keep our healthcare system alive. I know how hard they work, how much they sacrifice, and how urgently our state needs more advanced practice nurses — not fewer.
So when I saw this headline...
https://secure.ngpvan.com/4OI02pK92kOTd_uWTjBgFg2 [https://secure.ngpvan.com/4OI02pK92kOTd_uWTjBgFg2]
...I felt sick.
Under Donald Trump’s new proposal, advanced nursing degrees would no longer count as “professional,” meaning students would lose access to the federal loans they need to pursue graduate education. Loan limits would be slashed in half. Thousands of future nurse practitioners, nurse midwives, CRNAs, and clinical nurse specialists could be forced to take on massive private debt — or give up their dreams entirely.
This is not just bad policy.
It’s dangerous — for patients, for hospitals, and for the future of healthcare in this country.
Georgia already faces provider shortages. Rural communities already wait weeks for care. And now Trump wants to create even fewer advanced practice nurses?
We cannot allow this to go unanswered.
The American Nurses Association has already sounded the alarm, and more than 200,000 nurses and patients have raised their voices. Now I’m asking you to add yours:
Will you sign the petition demanding the Department of Education reverse this reckless decision and recognize advanced nursing degrees as the professional degrees they are? [https://secure.ngpvan.com/4OI02pK92kOTd_uWTjBgFg2]
ADD YOUR NAME [https://secure.ngpvan.com/4OI02pK92kOTd_uWTjBgFg2]
Our nurses deserve respect. Our students deserve opportunity. And our healthcare system deserves investment — not sabotage.
Thank you for standing up for science, for students, and for the people who keep us healthy.
Dr. Jasmine Clark
Democratic Candidate for Georgia's 13th District

| | Friend,If you were sitting across from me in an exam room, I wouldn’t give you a polished speech.I’d tell you the truth.Because that’s what you deserve. And here’s the truth:America isn’t just facing challenges; we’re ignoring the diagnosis.Washington keeps treating symptoms: More spending. More bureaucracy. More excuses.But the underlying problems? They’re getting worse.As a doctor, that’s unacceptable. So I wrote down exactly what I would tell you if we were having that conversation face-to-face.No politics. Just the truth about where we are... and what it’s going to take to fix it.

subject: I won’t stay silent on Gaza

body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-26q2?refcode=x_260403_gaza_actrs [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-26q2?refcode=x_260403_gaza_actrs]
Peter,
It turns out Democratic officials are afraid of admitting what we already know is true: Democrats lost to Trump in 2024 because of their support for the genocide in Gaza.
I’ve always stood up for the people in Gaza, and I haven’t stopped.
That’s why they spent $15 million to take me down last cycle, and it’s why I expect they’ll spend the same this year.
We can’t afford to sit back. My primary is a little over 4 months away, and every moment counts. We need people-powered campaigns that fight for justice, equity, and accountability.
Chip in today to fight back against corporate money and special interests before it’s too late >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-26q2?refcode=x_260403_gaza_actrs]

I’ve been calling to abolish ICE for years – while my opponent voted to thank them and took checks from ICE contractors.
St. Louis deserves leaders who do what’s right, not what’s profitable. Please support our campaign today. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-26q2?refcode=x_260403_gaza_actrs]

name: Team Moss
subject: Stand with Jeremy to protect affordable healthcare
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeremy Moss for Congress
body:
We’re at a critical moment for healthcare in this country. Here in Michigan, we feel it more than ever. Whether it’s our seniors, children, working families, or those who still don’t have proper insurance, too many of us are left behind by a broken system.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128705623/612106338/680287355?refcode=BLU_EM_MOSS_2026-04-03_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTc5OTIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZDZhODI2YTAtNzQyZi1mMTExLTlhNDgtMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=md7is3crqUdjOVBHeGL8BC81XuMjyuL6WLlkemRFgfE=)
---
| Peter,

We’re at a critical moment for healthcare in this country. Here in Michigan, we feel it more than ever. Whether it’s our seniors, children, working families, or those who still don’t have proper insurance, too many of us are left behind by a broken system.

**That’s why Jeremy is fighting to protect the Affordable Care Act by calling on Congress and President Trump to permanently extend the ACA Enhanced Premium Tax Credit. So that every Michigan family has equitable access to healthcare, regardless of their income.**

But we’re not out of the woods yet. Republicans in Congress are working overtime to undermine the ACA, and Trump’s push to gut healthcare is still very real. We can’t let them take us backwards.

Jeremy has always stood up to Trump – from healthcare to immigrant rights to basic human decency. Now, he’s fighting to make sure that everyone has access to the care they need. Because healthcare is a human right, not a luxury for the wealthy.

[_**Will you stand with Jeremy and sign our petition to tell Trump and Republicans: we won’t let them strip Michiganders of our healthcare?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128705624/612106339/775481550?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTc5OTIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZDZhODI2YTAtNzQyZi1mMTExLTlhNDgtMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=md7is3crqUdjOVBHeGL8BC81XuMjyuL6WLlkemRFgfE=&emci=d48aa3e2-d42e-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&emdi=d6a826a0-742f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&ceid=18274300)

clean_body: We’re at a critical moment for healthcare in this country. Here in Michigan, we feel it more than ever. Whether it’s our seniors, children, working families, or those who still don’t have proper insurance, too many of us are left behind by a broken system. | = | Peter, We’re at a critical moment for healthcare in this country. Here in Michigan, we feel it more than ever. Whether it’s our seniors, children, working families, or those who still don’t have proper insurance, too many of us are left behind by a broken system. That’s why Jeremy is fighting to protect the Affordable Care Act by calling on Congress and President Trump to permanently extend the ACA Enhanced Premium Tax Credit. So that every Michigan family has equitable access to healthcare, regardless of their income. But we’re not out of the woods yet. Republicans in Congress are working overtime to undermine the ACA, and Trump’s push to gut healthcare is still very real. We can’t let them take us backwards. Jeremy has always stood up to Trump – from healthcare to immigrant rights to basic human decency. Now, he’s fighting to make sure that everyone has access to the care they need. Because healthcare is a human right, not a luxury for the wealthy. | Thank you for standing with us as we continue to fight for Michigan families and protect the future of healthcare. Team Moss * | | = Paid for by Jeremy Moss for Congress Jeremy Moss for Congress 2211 S Telegraph Rd PO Box 7023 Bloomfield Hills, MI 48302 United States | | | ---|---|--- = =

Is there ANYTHING I can say to get you to split a donation between my campaign and Resist Reclaim Rebuild one last time before our end-of-month deadline on Saturday?
What if I told you Mike Johnson just announced a $65 MILLION warchest to buy a Republican House majority?
What if I told you Donald Trump is trying to rig our elections nationwide??
What if I told you we’re falling DANGEROUSLY short of our end-of month deadline? We still need to raise $24,428 TODAY.
These are the most consequential Midterm elections we've faced in years... Health care, cost of living, voting rights -- it's all on the line.
I’m counting on grassroots supporters like you to fight with me against these MAGA cowards. CNN says we CAN win back a Democratic House Majority. But to do it, we can’t let up for a single moment or else Trump and Mike Johnson will sense weakness, flood this district with MAGA cash, and drown our hopes of taking back the House for Democrats.
I apologize. I know you’ve heard a lot from me, and I ask a lot of you. But this is quite possibly my most urgent ask yet. If we don’t close this dangerous fundraising gap by SATURDAY AT MIDNIGHT, we could end up handing the House to Trump and giving him the power he craves.
If you care about flipping the House and saving our democracy, rush anything you can afford ASAP, before midnight on Saturday, to Resist Reclaim Rebuild and my campaign: [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-conway]
CHIP IN TO MY CAMPAIGN TODAY! >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-conway]
I hope you will.
Thank you,
George Conway

Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party's weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important facts - a kernel of truth - from the week. _[If you were forwarded this newsletter, you can sign up to receive the Kernel every week by clicking here.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128706462/612107615/1416943042?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5N2U1NGVlMS03NTJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=H7V-vKl81wkadD5LFXh6pvcyTqq11wCh01lSRybMNbs=&emci=91adfd8a-172f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&emdi=97e54ee1-752f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&ceid=4925535)_ [_**Donate to the INDems' 2026 Ready Fund today.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128706463/612107621/383765187?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5N2U1NGVlMS03NTJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=H7V-vKl81wkadD5LFXh6pvcyTqq11wCh01lSRybMNbs=) ****
_Voter Registration Deadline Monday, April 6_
[__](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128706464/612107622/-1499154792?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5N2U1NGVlMS03NTJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=H7V-vKl81wkadD5LFXh6pvcyTqq11wCh01lSRybMNbs=&emci=91adfd8a-172f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&emdi=97e54ee1-752f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&ceid=4925535) We are just days away from the deadline to register to vote in Indiana. Please head to [indianavoters.com](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128706465/612107623/2120319247?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5N2U1NGVlMS03NTJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=H7V-vKl81wkadD5LFXh6pvcyTqq11wCh01lSRybMNbs=&emci=91adfd8a-172f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&emdi=97e54ee1-752f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&ceid=4925535) today and double check your registration before the deadline on Monday, April 6. You can update your information, including changing your address if you moved since the last election in November 2024. Please take a few moments and head to [indianavoters.com](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128706467/612107625/2120319247?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5N2U1NGVlMS03NTJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=H7V-vKl81wkadD5LFXh6pvcyTqq11wCh01lSRybMNbs=&emci=91adfd8a-172f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&emdi=97e54ee1-752f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&ceid=4925535) to ensure you’re registered! Early voting begins Tuesday, April 7. ****_Gas Prices Soar Past $4 in Indiana and Nationwide_
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128706468/612107628/598072785?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5N2U1NGVlMS03NTJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=H7V-vKl81wkadD5LFXh6pvcyTqq11wCh01lSRybMNbs=&emci=91adfd8a-172f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&emdi=97e54ee1-752f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&ceid=4925535) Thanks to Trump, gas is now over $4/gallon. This is the single-largest one month price increase on gasoline in history. Despite this, Indiana’s Republican representatives in Congress still refuse to do their jobs and provide critical oversight of the war in Iran which is costing every American more. Hoosiers want an end to the war, and responsible policies that lower prices at the pump and create stability. ****_Each week, the State Party will highlight key policy from Democrats at the Statehouse or across the state. Democrats deliver a better future for Hoosier families, and we urge you to share this information with your networks._ [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128706469/612107629/213383120?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5N2U1NGVlMS03NTJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=H7V-vKl81wkadD5LFXh6pvcyTqq11wCh01lSRybMNbs=&emci=91adfd8a-172f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&emdi=97e54ee1-752f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&ceid=4925535) Indiana Democrats STAND with the United Steelworkers and the over 1,700 employees who were locked out by NIPSCO today. On Thursday, Congressman Frank Mrvan, State Rep. Randy Novak, State Senators Rodney Pol, Shelli Yoder, and JD Ford, along with IDP Chair Karen Tallian joined a rally of workers in Chesterton. NIPSCO is making record profits, but is increasingly hiring outside contractors and threatening workplace safety. NIPSCO must give its workers a fair contract. Over in Whiting, USW Local 7-1 members have been locked out by BP after pushing back against pay cuts and staff layoffs. We stand with them, and recognize the work they do to keep their communities safe. BP must give them a fair contract and end their illegal lockout. **[Click here to see other opportunities and party events near you on Mobilize!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128706470/612107630/1316591425?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5N2U1NGVlMS03NTJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=H7V-vKl81wkadD5LFXh6pvcyTqq11wCh01lSRybMNbs=&emci=91adfd8a-172f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&emdi=97e54ee1-752f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&ceid=4925535)** clean_body: | Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party's weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important facts - a kernel of truth - from the week. = ** Voter Registration Deadline Monday, April 6 We are just days away from the deadline to register to vote in Indiana. Please head to today and double check your registration before the deadline on Monday, April 6. You can update your information, including changing your address if you moved since the last election in November 2024. Please take a few moments and head to to ensure you’re registered! Early voting begins Tuesday, April 7. Gas Prices Soar Past $4 in Indiana and Nationwide Thanks to Trump, gas is now over $4/gallon. This is the single-largest one month price increase on gasoline in history. Despite this, Indiana’s Republican representatives in Congress still refuse to do their jobs and provide critical oversight of the war in Iran which is costing every American more. Hoosiers want an end to the war, and responsible policies that lower prices at the pump and create stability. Each week, the State Party will highlight key policy from Democrats at the Statehouse or across the state. Democrats deliver a better future for Hoosier families, and we urge you to share this information with your networks. Indiana Democrats STAND with the United Steelworkers and the over 1,700 employees who were locked out by NIPSCO today. On Thursday, Congressman Frank Mrvan, State Rep. Randy Novak, State Senators Rodney Pol, Shelli Yoder, and JD Ford, along with IDP Chair Karen Tallian joined a rally of workers in Chesterton. NIPSCO is making record profits, but is increasingly hiring outside contractors and threatening workplace safety. NIPSCO must give its workers a fair contract. Over in Whiting, USW Local 7-1 members have been locked out by BP after pushing back against pay cuts and staff layoffs. We stand with them, and recognize the work they do to keep their communities safe. BP must give them a fair contract and end their illegal lockout. Information on County Party events, Town Halls, protests, volunteer opportunities, and more can be found on our Upcoming Candidate Trainings Join the National Democratic Training Committee on Saturday, April 11th at 11 AM ET for a free virtual live training on Campaign Communications Bootcamp: Sharpen Your Message to Win. INDems IN the News Daily Journal: IndyStar: Indiana Capital Chronicle: * NWI Times: [content goes here] 101 W Washington St., #1110E Indianapolis, IN 46204 Paid for by the Indiana Democratic Party, www.indems.org. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.

Folks: Trump just fired Attorney General Pam Bondi. Washington is treating it like a tabloid story. I'm treating it like an accountability moment — and I’d like you with me. Bondi doesn't get to shirk accountability. Not after what happened on her watch. ➡️ [**ADD YOUR NAME: Demand Pam Bondi testify before Congress »**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128707013/612110081/1326834256?refcode=AU_MTH_EG_GEN_RES_AC_20260403_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNDhlMTc0NS03NjJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KzDIH4dDY0osDNzomsTGPcbL69MByx0796weuX3zuOM=&emci=bf8e1745-762f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&emdi=d48e1745-762f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&ceid=252045) Here's what she needs to answer for: Under her leadership, the Justice Department dropped more than **23,000 criminal investigations** — terrorism cases, child exploitation cases, white-collar fraud cases — to redirect those resources to target non-criminal migrants and their children. Her DOJ is illegally detaining lawful DACA recipients and has misled a federal court for over a year to justify arresting migrants at their own immigration hearings — then quietly admitted the legal justification never existed. At Trump's direction, she also went after his political enemies, including several of my Senate colleagues, and turned the Justice Department into a weapon to silence dissent. The cases fell apart. The questions haven’t gone away. And she was just weeks away from a congressional subpoena over the Epstein files when Trump pulled the plug — shielding Trump’s billionaire allies and entrenched political elites. Firing Pam Bondi doesn't erase any of that. It doesn't answer any of that. She needs to come before Congress and answer for it — _**under oath.**_ ➡️ [**SIGN THE PETITION: Pam Bondi must testify »**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128707014/612110083/1326834256?refcode=AU_MTH_EG_GEN_RES_AC_20260403_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNDhlMTc0NS03NjJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KzDIH4dDY0osDNzomsTGPcbL69MByx0796weuX3zuOM=&emci=bf8e1745-762f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&emdi=d48e1745-762f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&ceid=252045) The House Oversight Committee has already made clear her firing won't get her out of testifying. But the louder the public demand, the harder it is to ignore. [**Add your name. Share this with everyone you know. Let's make sure Pam Bondi knows that New Mexico — and this country — is watching.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128707016/612110084/1326834256?refcode=AU_MTH_EG_GEN_RES_AC_20260403_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNDhlMTc0NS03NjJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KzDIH4dDY0osDNzomsTGPcbL69MByx0796weuX3zuOM=&emci=bf8e1745-762f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&emdi=d48e1745-762f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&ceid=252045) Thank you, Martin

| | | | High school dropout. Waffle House waitress. First woman to graduate from The Citadel. Single mom. Fighter.

Nancy Mace wasn’t born into power. She worked for every inch of ground she’s gained.

At 17, she dropped out of high school and took a job slinging hash browns at Waffle House to survive. No silver spoon. No Ivy League connections. Just grit, grace, and a whole lot of graveyard shifts.

By the grace of God, she turned that struggle into a story no politician can touch. She made history as the first woman to graduate from The Citadel, South Carolina’s legendary military college. She built her own business. She raised two kids on her own. And when the Left came for everything she believed in, she stood her ground. Now, she’s running for Governor of South Carolina.

Nancy Mace is a battle-tested conservative who’s walked through fire and come out swinging. In Congress, she’s been one of President Trump’s fiercest allies, fighting to secure our border, protect our daughters, and stop the Left’s sick agenda dead in its tracks.

She’s not here to make friends. She’s here to make history.

Nancy is fighting to:

* Eliminate South Carolina’s Income Tax—the highest in the Southeast

* Prosecute violent criminals and pedophiles with zero leniency

* Crack down on sanctuary sheriffs who let illegal aliens roam free

* Defund radical public universities that indoctrinate, not educate

* Reform education so students learn how to think—not what to think

* Work hand-in-hand with President Trump to put South Carolina FIRST

Through every hardship, Nancy never gave up. And now she’s ready to do what she’s always done: roll up her sleeves, take the heat, and lead from the front.

South Carolina is worth fighting for. And Nancy Mace is just getting started.
---

name: NEGOP
subject: 🚨 Your Weekly Update From The NEGOP
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Nebraska Republican Party 1610 N St
body:
News, events, and action items to keep Nebraska red and growing strong.
| | | | |
---
| Letter from the Chair
---
Fellow Republicans,
There's a definite shift in the atmosphere as the May 12th primary approaches. The NEGOP is helping key districts to set up Days of Action. Yard signs are being printed and distributed. Mailers are being designed and readied for posting.
The election cycle has a definite rhythm, and this year is no different. Preparation is key and the NEGOP has been in preparation mode for several weeks to help campaigns and candidates in a variety of ways. Our walk app and data software are offered to and to available all Republican candidates without charge. We continue to offer training on a variety of topics. We're helping candidates with printing and mailing. County candidates are also contacting us for help with mail rates, yard signs, and information.
Ongoing county conventions are resulting in some leadership changes and onboarding new county chairmen is critical. We have several counties with very experienced chairmen and several with less experienced leadership. Assuring counties can navigate ways to help their candidates is crucial.
Building relationships is a big part of politics and the NEGOP is reaching out to potential new constituencies who are frustrated with the Democratic Party. For years, Democrats have made diversity their rallying cry. But when faced with a candidate who doesn't fall in lockstep with their extreme agenda, they make their position clear: diversity of thought, especially pro-life thought, is not welcome in the Democratic Party. In contrast, the Nebraska Republican Party is and will remain, a big tent party that respects differing perspectives and trusts Nebraskans to think for themselves.
All the while, we're preparing for our State Convention. We're contacting speakers, sponsors, candidates, officeholders and more. Delegates and Alternates are still being selected by their county delegates, and all the election results are being sent to the NEGOP so we can begin our credentialing process.
I am very proud of the work effort accomplished by the NEGOP staff and volunteers. We have a dedicated group of committed individuals who keep their eyes on the goal of electing Republicans. Fortunately, many excellent, hardworking candidates have been recruited and are out knocking doors and speaking to voters.
I encourage all our readers to find a way to engage in this important election cycle. We cannot allow complacency to set in. Walk with a candidate, make calls for a candidate, and/or donate to a candidate. Midterms are tough and we must be vigilant to keep radical Democrats who want to appear moderate from gaining ground in Nebraska.
As always, the NEGOP also appreciates your support so that we can continue offering "free" apps and services to our candidates. And so that we can continue our mission of sustaining a party structure for all Republicans in Nebraska.
Together, we win!

For Nebraska, Mary Jane Truemper Chairman, Nebraska Republican Party

| Update from Political Director Joe Hagerty...
GOTV is underway -- and we need all hands on deck.
The NEGOP and local county parties are going to be running door knocking days of action in key legislative districts across CD2, and between now and May 12th, our volunteers are the most important asset we have. We're on the ground connecting with Republican voters, making sure they know when, where, and how to vote in the Primary -- because an informed voter is a voter who actually shows up.
These legislative races are close. The margins are tight. And in cycles like this one, volunteer-driven turnout operations are what separate the wins from the near-misses. A few hours of your time between now and Primary Day can make a real difference for our candidates and for Nebraska.
Not sure where to vote or need to check your registration? Visit [vote.ne.gop](https://feyrpzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001hqC5lx5DZuRcBSzf50PXcaXe0ZGKaEJw9BdQO4axnLCRxCS194e3JhY6dDKlgbiipycOW9mHCeKvLX62yVuyjg-6YPBfuidXKT-jjoZ82uVoZoidxN32zhYIJePDEMbfiiZfc9RzK0aAELsC9Vex0Q==&c=Zn2S45Aoe4MLNUde8EoDTaNzjXg211D-cTsj3j9ujAGhjB-1QUYo5g==&ch=mfntToBL4_NaIisr0j6j4FzvMRqtV5YHC2FlWkBfnBDyv2oGX8amCg==) for everything you need heading into May 12th. Share it with your neighbors, your family, and anyone you know who needs to make a plan. And if you haven't voted yet -- make a plan today. Know your polling location, know your hours, and make May 12th a priority. Every Republican vote cast on Primary Day is a step toward winning Nebraska in November.
Ready to get involved? Here's your next opportunity: Join us on Friday, April 11th for a day of action in LD 36 supporting Rick Holdcroft. Canvassing starts at 10:00 AM -- grab coffee and donuts with the team at 9:30 before we head out.
