### HB 5350 Raises THC Limits -- and Questions About Jason Rojas's Support
| | [Jonathan Goldstein](https://substack.com/@jonathangoldstein81) and [Dr. Michael Goldstein](https://substack.com/@drmichaelgoldsteinct)
---
| Apr 3
---
| [](https://substack.com/@jonathangoldstein81)| | [](https://substack.com/@drmichaelgoldsteinct)
---|---|---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1454853&post_id=193068727&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTkzMDY4NzI3LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc3NTI0MTE2OCwiZXhwIjoxNzc3ODMzMTY4LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTQ1NDg1MyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.nM4XMOfGwMLpN6jBfDG8a2YEjxBtdjPCbdrcg3SXx-E&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1454853&post_id=193068727&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTkzMDY4NzI3LCJpYXQiOjE3NzUyNDExNjgsImV4cCI6MTc3NzgzMzE2OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE0NTQ4NTMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.yW5yhFdwBr4EUeIPJhy2wM7pJM0g1cs6WVjYQbPvilI&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1454853&post_id=193068727&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTkzMDY4NzI3LCJpYXQiOjE3NzUyNDExNjgsImV4cCI6MTc3NzgzMzE2OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE0NTQ4NTMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.yW5yhFdwBr4EUeIPJhy2wM7pJM0g1cs6WVjYQbPvilI)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvZHJtaWNoYWVsZ29sZHN0ZWluNGN0L3AvaGlnaGVyLXRoYy1oaWdoZXItc3Rha2VzLWhiLTUzNTA_.GuwSdT6zijW3izgVrUI9VUSoNvjGTftL5QB_krjMJJE?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/drmichaelgoldstein4ct/p/higher-thc-higher-stakes-hb-5350?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2ebcf0ef-e0bf-4260-87a8-9d1e0e761cae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
House Majority Leader Jason Rojas filed testimony stating that he strongly supports HB 5350 which reduces restrictions in the cannabis industry in Connecticut. In that testimony, he argued that the bill would reduce unnecessary regulatory barriers, increase product variety, and give Connecticut's adult-use cannabis industry a needed boost. He also stated that "**THC-infused beverages would be made available in social spaces, such as restaurants and bars** ," and said those changes were critical to drawing customers and retail sales back to Connecticut.
_**The cleanest conclusion is this: the issue is not $750 in campaign contributions to Rojas from a cannabis coalition that agreed to a $416,000 settlement. The issue is whether Connecticut cannabis policy is being shaped too comfortably around the interests of politically connected operators, even after the state Attorney General Tong warned that this market requires real guardrails.**_
_**This is not just a debate about cannabis policy. It is a debate about judgment, political proximity, and whether Connecticut is weakening guardrails at the same time the state itself is warning that the market needs them.**_
[ Pledge your support](https://substack..9J3BjyqRDKjAL_acDBA_hCSD0cvmP-6r9kIJk33Qvek?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=193068727)
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1454853&post_id=193068727&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTkzMDY4NzI3LCJpYXQiOjE3NzUyNDExNjgsImV4cCI6MTc3NzgzMzE2OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE0NTQ4NTMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.yW5yhFdwBr4EUeIPJhy2wM7pJM0g1cs6WVjYQbPvilI)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/aee1f41e-7587-4fab-81ef-8d9db41bcd4d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[Source - Rojas - CT House Page ](https://substack.com/redirect/1cb8cb48-1fc8-4aa4-9630-8da41acaed4d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
**Crisp Cannabis was not on the sidelines of HB 5350.**
Andrew Simonow, President of Crisp Cannabis, also submitted testimony on HB 5350. His testimony focused primarily on the proposed commercial extractor license structure. He argued that Connecticut already has a strong regulated cannabis supply chain, opposed creating a separate THC-production pathway outside the existing regulated system, and supported reasonable improvements to branding and packaging rules that would strengthen Connecticut's in-state cannabis market.
**Those two testimonies (Rojas and Simonow) matter even more when placed next to the Attorney General 's January 2026 settlement with Crisp Cannabis.**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ef539862-03b6-462a-b03a-9db98348d156?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[Source AG Tong Press Release Site](https://substack.com/redirect/3e0da548-ba09-45c0-a065-3e13aaa0a4ed?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The Attorney General announced a $416,000 settlement with three cannabis establishment licensees in Bridgeport, East Hartford, and Cromwell operating under the Crisp Cannabis brand, along with Mohave CT LLC and related principals. The state said the matter involved allegations of gun jumping, premature transfer of operational control, coordinated branding and staffing, and exchanges of competitively sensitive pricing and operational data before required review.
**Cannabis PAC Support for Rojas**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d352794c-1989-4de0-8495-3f288a39f818?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Then there is the PAC filing. The attached event activity report for the Jason Rojas PAC shows Sara's Dispensary, Ty's Dispensary, and Debbie's Dispensary listed as purchasers at $250 each, for a total of $750. Standing alone, that amount is not the main point. The point is that the same dispensary entities appearing on the PAC report were later tied to the state settlement, while Rojas submitted testimony supporting a bill that would loosen barriers in the cannabis market and Crisp itself was actively lobbying on the bill.
That sequence does not prove bribery or a quid pro quo. It does, however, create a serious appearance question. Why was a top legislative leader pushing HB 5350 while businesses later wrapped into one of the state's most serious cannabis enforcement actions were in his PAC orbit and while a related operator was advocating on the same legislation?
_**The issue is not three $250 PAC purchases by themselves. The issue is the pattern: Jason Rojas supported HB 5350, Crisp Cannabis also testified on HB 5350, and Rojas 's PAC report reflects support from the same three dispensary entities later tied to the state's cannabis and antitrust settlement.**_
**What About the Science? Or At Least the Health Concerns of the DPH and Others**
As April is Cannabis Awareness Month, Connecticut's own public-health data makes the timing more troubling. The Department of Public Health's 2025 Cannabis Health Statistics Report found that adult cannabis use increased from 2021 to 2023, that 14.5 percent of adults reported past-month use in 2023, and that cannabis-related poison-control cases among children under six increased sharply from 2019 to 2023. The report also found elevated concern around youth outcomes, depression, and adverse exposure trends.
That same body of data cuts directly against any attempt to dismiss youth exposure as a side issue. A Norwalk youth survey reported that the average age of first marijuana use among local youth who use marijuana is 14, that 69 Norwalk youth went to the emergency department from marijuana use in a single year, and that this was roughly double the number from five years earlier.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d84be4a9-957d-418d-8891-3da73b8ba78d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The potency issue is not academic. Connecticut's 2025 Department of Public Health report does not itself prove that raising THC concentration caps will increase psychotic episodes. _**But it does document a rising-use environment, substantial repeat use, and enough cannabis-related public-health concern that the state is planning future analysis of cannabis-related mental-health visits and cannabinoid hyperemesis syndrome.**_ Against that backdrop, broader national evidence points in a clear cautionary direction.
The U.S. Surgeon General has warned that the risk for psychotic disorders increases with frequency of use, potency of the marijuana product, and younger age at first use. The National Academies likewise have concluded that cannabis use is likely to increase the risk of schizophrenia and other psychoses, with heavier use associated with greater risk. Federal coding materials also recognize cannabinoid hyperemesis syndrome, or CHS, as an emerging condition marked by cyclical nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain after prolonged, frequent cannabis use, and explain that higher THC concentrations have not been tied to CHS directly but are likely to increase chronic use, which in turn increases CHS risk.
[Many of these issues were raised by Nicole Hampton, a community advocate who works with individuals and families impacted by addiction and mental health challenges, who opposed HB5350](https://substack.com/redirect/16ace68d-f0de-4b96-a46c-0619278a546e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
_**So when lawmakers talk about lifting concentration caps, they are not just talking about more consumer choice. They are talking about a policy change that may intensify the exact pattern of heavy, high-potency use associated in the medical literature with greater psychosis risk and with the chronic-use profile linked to CHS.**_
So the contradiction is hard to miss. The Attorney General says this market needs competition safeguards. The Department of Public Health says cannabis-related risks remain serious. Yet HB 5350 is promoted as a bill to make the market more flexible, more commercially attractive, and more socially normalized.
**Public Records Context**
1. [January 2026 Attorney General settlement announcement involving the Crisp Cannabis network, Mohave CT LLC, and related principals.](https://substack.com/redirect/cf73c1b8-aa1d-4441-9d94-9c3fd08ee1a1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
2. [Connecticut Department of Public Health 2025 Cannabis Health Statistics Report.](https://substack.com/redirect/e702c54c-59b4-40bf-aa0c-ca627f1457dd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
3. [HB 5350 public materials concerning cannabis, hemp, and infused beverage regulation.](https://substack.com/redirect/f229730b-1530-456e-b9be-5dec0957d7d1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
4. [Testimony of House Majority Leader Rojas ](https://substack.com/redirect/0a5ea82c-e3cf-458c-8bd1-7af10cb18178?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
5. [Testimony of Andrew Simonov - President Crisp Cannabis](https://substack.com/redirect/52189a01-af27-4dc2-8109-7d6412befa5e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

_**Bottom Line**_
_**Rojas backed the bill. Crisp lobbied the bill. Rojas 's PAC report reflects support from the same dispensary entities later tied to the settlement. That may not establish wrongdoing, but it is more than enough to justify harder scrutiny, direct questions, and a demand for full transparency.**_
| A guest post by| [Jonathan Goldstein](https://substack.com/@jonathangoldstein81?utm_campaign=guest_post_bio&utm_medium=email)Business Consultant, Political Consultant, Campaign Manager, Construction Consultant and Corporate Attorney. Jonathan served as the Campaign Manager for Dr. Michael Goldstein - CT-04 [Subscribe to Jonathan](https://substack.com/redirect/5863669c-2cb1-4220-b373-994866e95e6d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

clean_body: HB 5350 Raises THC Limits -- and Questions About Jason Rojas's Support | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more The Goldstein Substack - A Father-Son Publication Dr. Goldstein is Running for Congress and his page can be found HB 5350 Raises THC Limits -- and Questions About Jason Rojas's Support | | and | Apr 3 | | | ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- House Majority Leader Jason Rojas filed testimony stating that he strongly supports HB 5350 which reduces restrictions in the cannabis industry in Connecticut. In that testimony, he argued that the bill would reduce unnecessary regulatory barriers, increase product variety, and give Connecticut's adult-use cannabis industry a needed boost. He also stated that "THC-infused beverages would be made available in social spaces, such as restaurants and bars ," and said those changes were critical to drawing customers and retail sales back to Connecticut. The cleanest conclusion is this: the issue is not $750 in campaign contributions to Rojas from a cannabis coalition that agreed to a $416,000 settlement. The issue is whether Connecticut cannabis policy is being shaped too comfortably around the interests of politically connected operators, even after the state Attorney General Tong warned that this market requires real guardrails. This is not just a debate about cannabis policy. It is a debate about judgment, political proximity, and whether Connecticut is weakening guardrails at the same time the state itself is warning that the market needs them. | | ---|---|--- Crisp Cannabis was not on the sidelines of HB 5350. Andrew Simonow, President of Crisp Cannabis, also submitted testimony on HB 5350. His testimony focused primarily on the proposed commercial extractor license structure. He argued that Connecticut already has a strong regulated cannabis supply chain, opposed creating a separate THC-production pathway outside the existing regulated system, and supported reasonable improvements to branding and packaging rules that would strengthen Connecticut's in-state cannabis market. Those two testimonies (Rojas and Simonow) matter even more when placed next to the Attorney General 's January 2026 settlement with Crisp Cannabis. | | ---|---|--- The Attorney General announced a $416,000 settlement with three cannabis establishment licensees in Bridgeport, East Hartford, and Cromwell operating under the Crisp Cannabis brand, along with Mohave CT LLC and related principals. The state said the matter involved allegations of gun jumping, premature transfer of operational control, coordinated branding and staffing, and exchanges of competitively sensitive pricing and operational data before required review. Cannabis PAC Support for Rojas | | ---|---|--- Then there is the PAC filing. The attached event activity report for the Jason Rojas PAC shows Sara's Dispensary, Ty's Dispensary, and Debbie's Dispensary listed as purchasers at $250 each, for a total of $750. Standing alone, that amount is not the main point. The point is that the same dispensary entities appearing on the PAC report were later tied to the state settlement, while Rojas submitted testimony supporting a bill that would loosen barriers in the cannabis market and Crisp itself was actively lobbying on the bill. That sequence does not prove bribery or a quid pro quo. It does, however, create a serious appearance question. Why was a top legislative leader pushing HB 5350 while businesses later wrapped into one of the state's most serious cannabis enforcement actions were in his PAC orbit and while a related operator was advocating on the same legislation? The issue is not three $250 PAC purchases by themselves. The issue is the pattern: Jason Rojas supported HB 5350, Crisp Cannabis also testified on HB 5350, and Rojas 's PAC report reflects support from the same three dispensary entities later tied to the state's cannabis and antitrust settlement. What About the Science? Or At Least the Health Concerns of the DPH and Others As April is Cannabis Awareness Month, Connecticut's own public-health data makes the timing more troubling. The Department of Public Health's 2025 Cannabis Health Statistics Report found that adult cannabis use increased from 2021 to 2023, that 14.5 percent of adults reported past-month use in 2023, and that cannabis-related poison-control cases among children under six increased sharply from 2019 to 2023. The report also found elevated concern around youth outcomes, depression, and adverse exposure trends. That same body of data cuts directly against any attempt to dismiss youth exposure as a side issue. A Norwalk youth survey reported that the average age of first marijuana use among local youth who use marijuana is 14, that 69 Norwalk youth went to the emergency department from marijuana use in a single year, and that this was roughly double the number from five years earlier. | | ---|---|--- The potency issue is not academic. Connecticut's 2025 Department of Public Health report does not itself prove that raising THC concentration caps will increase psychotic episodes. But it does document a rising-use environment, substantial repeat use, and enough cannabis-related public-health concern that the state is planning future analysis of cannabis-related mental-health visits and cannabinoid hyperemesis syndrome. Against that backdrop, broader national evidence points in a clear cautionary direction. The U.S. Surgeon General has warned that the risk for psychotic disorders increases with frequency of use, potency of the marijuana product, and younger age at first use. The National Academies likewise have concluded that cannabis use is likely to increase the risk of schizophrenia and other psychoses, with heavier use associated with greater risk. Federal coding materials also recognize cannabinoid hyperemesis syndrome, or CHS, as an emerging condition marked by cyclical nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain after prolonged, frequent cannabis use, and explain that higher THC concentrations have not been tied to CHS directly but are likely to increase chronic use, which in turn increases CHS risk. So when lawmakers talk about lifting concentration caps, they are not just talking about more consumer choice. They are talking about a policy change that may intensify the exact pattern of heavy, high-potency use associated in the medical literature with greater psychosis risk and with the chronic-use profile linked to CHS. So the contradiction is hard to miss. The Attorney General says this market needs competition safeguards. The Department of Public Health says cannabis-related risks remain serious. Yet HB 5350 is promoted as a bill to make the market more flexible, more commercially attractive, and more socially normalized. Public Records Context 1. 2. 3. 4. 5. Bottom Line Rojas backed the bill. Crisp lobbied the bill. Rojas 's PAC report reflects support from the same dispensary entities later tied to the settlement. That may not establish wrongdoing, but it is more than enough to justify harder scrutiny, direct questions, and a demand for full transparency.** | A guest post by| Consultant, Political Consultant, Campaign Manager, Construction Consultant and Corporate Attorney. Jonathan served as the Campaign Manager for Dr. Michael Goldstein - CT-04 The Goldstein Substack is free today. But if you enjoyed this post, you can tell The Goldstein Substack that their writing is valuable by pledging a future subscription. You won't be charged unless they enable payments. | | | | | | | (C) 2026 Michael Goldstein ? ?

| Team, this week, **President Trump signed an executive order that represents a direct attack on the fundamental right of every American to cast their vote and have it counted.**

This order is not about election integrity; it is a massive and unconstitutional voter suppression effort spearheaded by President Trump. Ultimately, this is about silencing people.

Oregon has been a national leader in vote-by-mail since we became the first state to conduct all elections entirely by mail in 2000. We will not let an unconstitutional power grab change that.

[We must meet this moment with the urgency it deserves. name: Scott Peters
subject: This isn’t about politics
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scott Peters for Congress
body:
---
| | Peter – This might not be the flashiest issue we deal with in Washington, but it’s one of the most important: **our national debt and deficit.**

For decades, Washington has made decisions that added to our debt – and now, with rising interest rates, **younger Americans are the ones being left to pick up the tab.**

That’s not right.

Right now, the United States is running deficits of nearly $2 trillion a year, and we’re spending close to $1 trillion annually just on interest payments.

**That’s money we’re not investing in schools, infrastructure, or helping people afford housing, health care, and everyday costs.**

[If you agree Congress must take real, responsible action to reduce the deficit and protect the next generation, add your name.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128721779/612146868/103900609?refcode2=3a80dd85-8c2f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&refcode=SP_EM_PT_90DO_260403_1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzkvMS84ODA2MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYTgwZGQ4NS04YzJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kVRt40Ya2PGiw6qYGi9df1dYltYKyLdWj9jT4V8aX5g=&emci=6ea06df0-892f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&emdi=3a80dd85-8c2f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&ceid=5258741) | | **[ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128721781/612146870/103900609?refcode2=3a80dd85-8c2f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&refcode=SP_EM_PT_90DO_260403_1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzkvMS84ODA2MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYTgwZGQ4NS04YzJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kVRt40Ya2PGiw6qYGi9df1dYltYKyLdWj9jT4V8aX5g=&emci=6ea06df0-892f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&emdi=3a80dd85-8c2f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&ceid=5258741)**
---
This isn’t a partisan issue, Peter. I’m working across the aisle to establish a clear, responsible fiscal target: **reducing our deficit to 3 percent of our economy.**

It’s a practical, achievable step that would help stabilize our debt and restore accountability to how we budget and spend.

Because right now, young people are being asked to carry the weight of decisions they didn’t make. That’s not fair, and it’s not responsible. We owe them better than that.

**We can still make changes to get back on track, but it will take serious, bipartisan work and a commitment to long-term solutions.**

[Join me in calling for fiscal responsibility and a fairer future for the next generation.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128721784/612146872/103900609?refcode2=3a80dd85-8c2f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&refcode=SP_EM_PT_90DO_260403_1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzkvMS84ODA2MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYTgwZGQ4NS04YzJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kVRt40Ya2PGiw6qYGi9df1dYltYKyLdWj9jT4V8aX5g=&emci=6ea06df0-892f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&emdi=3a80dd85-8c2f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&ceid=5258741) | **[ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128721786/612146875/103900609?refcode2=3a80dd85-8c2f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&refcode=SP_EM_PT_90DO_260403_1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzkvMS84ODA2MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYTgwZGQ4NS04YzJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kVRt40Ya2PGiw6qYGi9df1dYltYKyLdWj9jT4V8aX5g=&emci=6ea06df0-892f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&emdi=3a80dd85-8c2f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&ceid=5258741)**
---
Thanks for staying engaged on this important issue,

Scott

clean_body: | | Peter – This might not be the flashiest issue we deal with in Washington, but it’s one of the most important: our national debt and deficit. For decades, Washington has made decisions that added to our debt – and now, with rising interest rates, younger Americans are the ones being left to pick up the tab. That’s not right. Right now, the United States is running deficits of nearly $2 trillion a year, and we’re spending close to $1 trillion annually just on interest payments. That’s money we’re not investing in schools, infrastructure, or helping people afford housing, health care, and everyday costs. | | This isn’t a partisan issue, Peter. I’m working across the aisle to establish a clear, responsible fiscal target: reducing our deficit to 3 percent of our economy. It’s a practical, achievable step that would help stabilize our debt and restore accountability to how we budget and spend. Because right now, young people are being asked to carry the weight of decisions they didn’t make. That’s not fair, and it’s not responsible. We owe them better than that. We can still make changes to get back on track, but it will take serious, bipartisan work and a commitment to long-term solutions. | Thanks for staying engaged on this important issue, Scott | | | = | As a leader among Democrats in Congress, Scott Peters has one of the strongest track records of achieving progressive reforms and getting things done. He’s committed to protecting our environment, improving access to affordable healthcare, implementing compassionate immigration reforms and protecting reproductive freedom. Democrats in swing districts like Scott’s rely on grassroots supporters like you.**= | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Paid for by Scott Peters for Congress Scott Peters for Congress PO Box 22074 San Diego, CA 92192 United States

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 4/3/2026
body:
“Single most insecure person I’ve ever encountered”: Former Griswold aide breaks silence ahead of AG race
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
| | Happy Friday!
Listen to the Mountain Minute podcast [here](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), or watch on [YouTube](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) or [Rumble](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.). And also, check out our [deep-dive video of the day](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) on Griswold's former aide breaking silence ahead of her AG race.
Here is a summary of today's top stories:
🏛️ **Under the Golden Dome...** Lawmakers are weighing major Medicaid cuts as part of efforts to close a significant budget gap, with warnings that [services for vulnerable residents could be sharply reduced](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) amid broader debates over spending priorities.

🏔️ **Here in Colorado...** A federal judge struck down the state’s [75% requirement to opt out of open primaries](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), while officials continue [challenging a new federal election order](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) as an overreach, and an appeals court [upheld Tina Peters’ convictions but ordered resentencing](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.). New scrutiny is emerging in the attorney general race after a [former Griswold aide raised concerns about internal leadership](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.). At the same time, scrutiny is growing over Colorado Parks and Wildlife after commissioners [advanced a petition backed by an out-of-state group](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) despite internal opposition.
Regulators warn [ongoing outages and delayed repairs](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) at Xcel’s Comanche 3 coal plant could strain electricity supply during peak summer demand. Debate also continues over [Denver’s push for higher-density housing](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) and its impact on infrastructure and quality of life, as well as criticism that federal permitting laws like NEPA have created [lengthy delays and higher costs for infrastructure projects](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.).
🇺🇸**On the National Stage...** Election and constitutional debates are intensifying, with critics questioning [why states share voter data with outside groups](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) but resist federal verification efforts tied to the SAVE Act, while the Supreme Court signaled [skepticism toward ending birthright citizenship](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), citing long-standing constitutional precedent.
This is your Mountain Minute for Friday, April 3, 2026.
– Team RMV
---
|
---
**TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
**[“Single most insecure person I’ve ever encountered”: Former Griswold aide breaks silence ahead of AG race](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
Reese Edwards didn’t plan to speak out.
He spent less than a year inside Colorado’s Secretary of State’s office before leaving in 2020, frustrated and burned out enough to walk away.
He wasn’t looking to revisit any of it. Now, with Jena Griswold running for attorney general, he’s speaking up—and doing it on the record.
“I love democracy with an almost religious belief,” he told RMV. “The idea of people deciding for themselves how they will govern themselves. That’s why I took the job.”
Edwards, who served as Director of Government and Public Affairs, is now going on record with a warning. He says what he witnessed inside the Secretary of State’s office—erratic leadership, blurred lines between political ambition and public duty and a culture that discouraged dissent—raises serious questions about whether Griswold is equipped to lead the state’s top legal office.
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Tina Peters convictions upheld, sentence thrown out and case sent back to Mesa County court](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* **Convictions upheld, sentence thrown out:** A Colorado appeals court upheld all convictions against former Mesa County Clerk Tina Peters but ordered resentencing, finding the trial judge improperly considered her protected speech.
* **Key legal arguments rejected:** The court dismissed claims that a presidential pardon could apply to state convictions and ruled Peters was not acting under federal authority, leaving the jury’s findings intact.
* **Case returns for resentencing:** The ruling sends the case back to Mesa County, where a judge must resentence Peters without factoring in her statements, while affirming limits on using First Amendment-protected speech in sentencing decisions.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
|
---
**[Denver’s growth dilemma: More housing, less breathing room](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY NEIL WOLKODOFF, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* **Density-driven housing push raises quality-of-life concerns:** The commentary argues Denver’s rapid expansion of high-density housing is changing the city’s character, replacing open space with large apartment complexes and reducing access to green areas.
* **Air quality and health impacts highlighted:** Increased density is linked to worsening air quality in Denver’s basin geography, with “poor” air quality days rising sharply and pollutant levels exceeding safe standards, contributing to health concerns.
* **Infrastructure strain and resource limits emerging:** The piece warns that higher density is stressing utilities, internet capacity, and water supply—potentially leading to brownouts, slower service, and stricter water restrictions as population and demand grow.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️ **
**[Federal order puts Colorado’s mail ballot system on a collision course](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY JEN SCHUMANN | STATE | APRIL 1** _The way Colorado runs elections hasn’t changed—but a new federal order could force it to. The order would have federal agencies assemble a nationwide list of verified U.S. citizens and share it with state election officials._
__
**[Colorado Legislative Malpractice](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY MICHAEL HANCOCK | COMMENTARY | APRIL 1** _There is a reason malpractice carries such moral weight in medicine. A physician is entrusted with the care of a patient. When that trust is violated—through negligence, arrogance, or ideological blindness—the consequences are not abstract. They are physical, measurable, and often irreversible.___
**[The Plain Truth Behind Voter ID, the SAVE America Act and Election Integrity](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY RUSS MINARY | COMMENTARY | APRIL 1** _“People who love sausage and respect the law should never watch either being made.” (Mark Twain). Congress has been dithering on a number of issues that impact legal, law-abiding US citizens and taxpayers. House and Senate reps from both parties are dragging their feet for various reasons._ __

**Feel like you have a unique story, commentary or tip to share?** Email [submit@rockymountainvoice.com](mailto:submit@rockymountainvoice.com).
|
---
|
---
**✝️ DID YOU KNOW?** **Good Friday’s name** is widely believed to come from an older meaning of “good” as “holy,” referring to its religious significance. Others say it reflects the belief that Jesus’ crucifixion was a sacrificial act that brought salvation. In some countries, traditions remain solemn—for example, Germany observes Good Friday as a “quiet day,” where dancing in public venues is legally restricted.
|
---
**CARTOON OF THE DAY**
|
---
**QUOTE OF THE DAY** "Sacrifice is the highest form of love." — Unknown
|
---

name: David Hogg
subject: This is why TSA lines are so long
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
Christian Menefee didn’t go to the airport to catch a flight — he went to listen to his constituents and tell them what’s really causing this chaos.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20260403-1&t=1&akid=3331%2E587540%2EK40JTj)
Peter,
Last week, Leaders We Deserve-endorsed candidate **Rep. Christian Menefee** stood in line with travelers and his constituents at Houston’s main airport.
Not to catch a flight — to listen.
And to show people exactly what’s causing this chaos.
[](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260403_1_menefee.rUKNdl&refcode=em20260403-3331&t=2&akid=3331%2E587540%2EK40JTj)
**The government has been partially shut down for 50 days now.** Nearly 500 TSA officers have had to quit because they’ve gone weeks without pay, and thousands more are struggling to afford rent, gas, and to put food on their tables.
On top of all that, Congress just left town for a two week recess.
They took off with no deal for unpaid TSA workers who have now missed two full paychecks, and with no remorse as they leave travelers stranded in lines that have hit the highest wait times in TSA history.
**Democrats have been clear — they want to fund TSA, but the GOP has blocked any attempt to do so _nine times_ so far. **
Make no mistake, Peter. They’re holding TSA workers’ paychecks hostage to protect the rogue agency that killed Renee Good, Alex Pretti, and Keith Porter in broad daylight. **They want more money for ICE so they can continue letting them terrorize our communities.**
_That_ is the kind of bullshit we’re dealing with.
Listen, Christian didn’t have to show up at the airport. He did so because that’s what real leadership looks like. Not tweeting, not sending out press releases, but standing shoulder to shoulder with the people who are being failed by this government _again_ , and making it clear who he’s fighting for.
We need more of that energy in Washington. _A lot more._
**[Will you pitch in $5 today to help Leaders We Deserve elect more unapologetic progressive fighters like Christian?](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260403_1_menefee.rUKNdl&refcode=em20260403-3331&amount=5&t=3&akid=3331%2E587540%2EK40JTj)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260403_1_menefee.rUKNdl&amount=5&refcode=em20260403-3331&t=22&akid=3331%2E587540%2EK40JTj)**
Thank you,
David Hogg
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20260403-1&t=23&refcode2=3331_587540_K40JTj&akid=3331%2E587540%2EK40JTj)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=24&akid=3331%2E587540%2EK40JTj)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=25&akid=3331%2E587540%2EK40JTj)

To give by check:
Leaders We Deserve
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE
#192
Washington, DC 20003
Email us: [info@leaderswedeserve.com](mailto:info@leaderswedeserve.com)
PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE.COM, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128724637/612153437/-1978189107?refcode=AU_XBCA_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20260403_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDcvMS8xMDYyNjIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMzdkNGJjMWMtOTAyZi1mMTExLTlhNDgtMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=t99zVRGd9ea-XdnCWIg8aGP3MPH5YfzaKYkf9huldEo=) **Donald Trump just fired his own Attorney General, Pam Bondi.** Why? She wasn't loyal enough. Because she couldn't deliver the politically motivated indictments he demanded. And she fumbled his administration's own Epstein scandal. This is what unchecked power looks like — and it should alarm every Californian who cares about the rule of law. Bondi spent 14 months turning the Justice Department into the president's personal law firm. Her firing doesn't erase that — and it doesn't end the threat. She was always just a symptom of Trump’s corruption. She still needs to answer for her conduct, and so will her replacement, Trump’s own former personal lawyer, Todd Blanche. When loyalty replaces accountability, investigations get buried, families get hurt, and the rule of law becomes negotiable. That kind of precedent doesn’t stay in Washington. It reaches into our courts, our schools, and our communities. California needs a governor who knows how to hold Trump accountable — a leader who will protect our institutions and keep our state from being dragged into political chaos. That is exactly why I'm in this race. clean_body: | = Donald Trump just fired his own Attorney General, Pam Bondi. Why? She wasn't loyal enough. Because she couldn't deliver the politically motivated indictments he demanded. And she fumbled his administration's own Epstein scandal. This is what unchecked power looks like — and it should alarm every Californian who cares about the rule of law. Bondi spent 14 months turning the Justice Department into the president's personal law firm. Her firing doesn't erase that — and it doesn't end the threat. She was always just a symptom of Trump’s corruption. She still needs to answer for her conduct, and so will her replacement, Trump’s own former personal lawyer, Todd Blanche. When loyalty replaces accountability, investigations get buried, families get hurt, and the rule of law becomes negotiable. That kind of precedent doesn’t stay in Washington. It reaches into our courts, our schools, and our communities. California needs a governor who knows how to hold Trump accountable — a leader who will protect our institutions and keep our state from being dragged into political chaos. That is exactly why I'm in this race. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Thanks for your support. name: Wes Moore
subject: My story
body:
# [](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/8?t=1&akid=1258%2E1086043%2EsLXtcS)
**_“Leave No One Behind.”_**
Peter, you hear it from me a lot. It’s the slogan I ran on, and it’s the first thing you see on welcome signs when driving into the great state of Maryland.
The story behind those words started when I joined the Army at 17. I wanted to follow in the footsteps of my mentors from military school. _I was still a minor, so my mom had to sign the paperwork for me to join._
I enlisted because I wanted to follow in the footsteps of my mentors in military school. They had shown me how to be accountable for others and overcome big challenges. When fellow soldiers needed our help, it was never a question of where they’re from or what party they vote for. We were all Americans, working toward a shared goal, and you **Leave No One Behind.**
To me, that’s true patriotism. Believing that the people around you -- your fellow soldiers, your neighbors, and your community -- deserve support now matter what.
It’s stuck with me, even years after hanging up my uniform. It’s how I work with people across Maryland to make this the best place in the world to change the world. When we’re able to embrace each other -- just like how I embraced what I learned in military school and in the Army -- we can create a future where no one is left behind.
As Maryland’s Governor, I promise to fight for that future.
Thanks for reading.
Elevate,
Wes

* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

BY AUTHORITY: WES MOORE FOR MARYLAND, MARY TYDINGS, TREASURER

name: Scott Presler
subject: The SAVE America Act would save the midterms, but not if the Senate doesn’t pass it
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Early Vote Action PAC
body:
| Patriot, I'll be honest with you. I've been watching the debate over the SAVE America Act in Washington — and while I'm cheering for it to pass, I've been in this fight long enough to know something most people won't say: **We cannot afford to wait on Washington to save our elections.** While politicians debate, the Democrat ground game does not stop. Their voter registration staffers are at their tables right now. Their ballot chasers are making their calls. Their door knockers are hitting their lists. _And if we are not doing the same thing — right now — we are falling behind._ **I know this because I've lived it.** In 2024, my team spent over a year embedded on the ground in Pennsylvania before Election Day. We helped register hundreds of thousands of new Republican voters. We chased down every outstanding mail-in ballot we legally could. We drove voters to the polls on Election Day. **That is why Pennsylvania went red.** Not because of any law. Not because of any court ruling. Because of boots on the ground, day after day, doing the unglamorous, exhausting, absolutely essential work of grassroots organizing.
---

Friend,

_**[Is there ANYTHING I can say to convince you to donate ANY amount before my fundraising filing deadline and help me win the New Mexico governor's race?](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/7253?refcode=jtk515-fr-nd&t=2&akid=8713%2E400544%2EdUMoqL)**_

---
What if I told you that my campaign is operating with fewer resources than we need to compete in one of the largest states in the country?

What if I told you that Democratic governors are essential to serve as a backstop when the federal government abuses its power and undermines civil liberties?

_And what if I told you that national Republicans are already eyeing this race and watching to see if we have the grassroots power to win?_
I'm in this race to lower costs, invest in education, and make healthcare more accessible and affordable, friend. But I can't win and deliver on these promises without your support —**especially ahead of my FIRST fundraising deadline before the primary.**
_[**Please, can I count on you to chip in $5, or anything at all, to help me reach my $150,000 goal, win in New Mexico, and move us toward a future where everyone can thrive?**](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/7253?refcode=jtk515-fr-nd&t=5&akid=8713%2E400544%2EdUMoqL)_
| | [Donate](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/7253?refcode=jtk515-fr-nd&t=6&akid=8713%2E400544%2EdUMoqL)
---
Be fierce,

Deb

name: Vice President JD Vance
subject: God led me to share my story with the world: warts and all!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC
body:
This is a longer email, but I respectfully ask you to read it start to finish:
[](https://links.republicanswinseats.-qcL2ezgA)My fellow Americans - it is Vice President JD Vance.
[This is a longer email, but I respectfully ask you to read it start to finish.](https://links.republicanswinseats.-qcL2ezgA)
It wasn't easy telling the world I grew up dirt poor, I was raised by my Mamaw while my mother struggled with addiction.
It wasn't exactly the prep school/trust fund/summer-in-the-Hamptons upbringing that is commonplace in DC.
But I felt God called me to tell my story. Warts and all. I felt led to speak out because I believe millions of Americans share stories like mine. Stories of jobs being outsourced to China, addiction ravaging communities and killing our neighbors, and heeding the call of duty after the Towers fell on 9/11.
As your Vice President, I now stand in the breach for the left-behind, forgotten men and women who built this country, but were abandoned by the political class in DC.
I'll do everything in my power to make sure that never happens again. I'll fight every day for good folks like you.
I promise to always stand between you and the Democrat Machine that aims to obliterate everything that makes our country great.
[Which brings me to the purpose of my email today.](https://links.republicanswinseats.-qcL2ezgA)
We're coming out of the most expensive election in American history. I mean that. Kamala Harris spent over a billion dollars against us. It took EVERY PENNY we had to get our story out there, expose her ultra-Liberal record, reach undecided voters, and win.
So now we're forced to rebuild.
The Democrat Machine is ALREADY crushing us in fundraising, ALREADY outspending us in key races, and ALREADY building the campaign infrastructure to decimate us in the Midterm Elections.
At this point, the consequences of simply resting on our laurels would be unthinkable.
So here's my final pitch to you:
[I'm teaming up with Majority Leader Steve Scalise to raise a million dollars by the end of the month.](https://links.republicanswinseats.-qcL2ezgA) I understand that's ambitious. But if that's what it takes to deliver President Trump a Republican House for his entire second term, it's a no-brainer. I'll do what it takes to win.
Do you think you can help us get there?
Whether it's $5 or $500 - the amount you give isn't nearly as important as how many folks step up: [PLEASE JOIN US
](https://links.republicanswinseats.-qcL2ezgA)Look, you've probably heard bits and pieces of my story at this point.
If my humble beginnings taught me anything, it's to never give up, never surrender, and never go down without a fight. That's why I'm partnering with Steve Scalise to help lay the critical groundwork we need to win.
[I don't ever want you to donate more than you can afford - but if you can spare ANYTHING, even a few bucks, it would be tremendously appreciated.](https://links.republicanswinseats.-qcL2ezgA)
Regardless, it's an honor to fight alongside you.
God bless America, Vice President JD Vance[PLEASE JOIN US
](https://links.republicanswinseats.-qcL2ezgA)
* * *
[](https://links.republicanswinseats.)\- Democrat Leadership formed an _anti-Trump task force_
\- More than a dozen Attorneys General are _suing_ the Trump Administration
\- A House Democrat just introduced _7 Articles of Impeachment_ _
_If they manage to strip us of our House Majority, they 'll have the power to impeach, investigate, and bring his entire legislative agenda to a SCREECHING HALT!
**We're PLEADING WITH YOU to do your part to help SAVE our Pro-Trump House Majority >>**[ DO YOUR PART
](https://links.republicanswinseats.)| | |
---
| | Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC.
---
Team Scalise is committed to restoring a Conservative House majority and electing Conservatives across the country. Thank you for being a part of our movement. This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because you are a member of the Team Scalise Conservative grassroots network.

Contributions to TEAM SCALISE, or any participating committee, are not deductible as charitable contributions for federal income tax purposes. Contributions from corporations, national banks, labor unions, foreign nationals without "green cards," and federal government contractors are prohibited. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, mailing address, occupation, and employer of each individual who contributes over $200 in a calendar year.

You can donate via mail by sending a check to:
320 First Street SE
2nd Floor
Washington, DC 20003

[Privacy Policy](https://teamscalise.com/privacy-policy/?recurring=true&amount=45&utm_campaign=20260401_GI-0H.118289_t1853398-1756&ex_tid=20260401_GI-0H.118289_t1853398-1756)

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128725634/612155825/1538324755?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwODZiZDdlZi05MjJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bCAu5oZ7IneFb65gxH-Z3OTtqBVH94uLSTK2Axgzopo=) Peter, President Trump calls vote-by-mail “cheating” — _yet he voted by mail in a recent Florida election._ **[In a moment, I’ll explain more about what Trump’s hypocritical attacks on mail-in voting mean for the future of our state.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128725635/612155826/-6070544?amounts=5,15,25,50,100,250,500&refcode=email_040326_votebymail&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwODZiZDdlZi05MjJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bCAu5oZ7IneFb65gxH-Z3OTtqBVH94uLSTK2Axgzopo=) [_But, if you’re ready to get on with your Friday or ready to take action to stand up to Trump with me, then I’m asking you to chip in today to help me protect Oregon’s vote-by-mail system and stand strong against these dangerous

Oregonians have voted by mail for over 25 years. It’s secure and accessible, _and it works._
Trump knows this — it’s why he voted by mail himself! But he doesn’t care about security or accessibility. He cares about unlawfully influencing elections to stay in power.
It’s why he and his allies are determined to undermine trust in voting. **[But, as your governor, with your help, I’m going to fight back!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128725638/612155829/-6070544?amounts=5,15,25,50,100,250,500&refcode=email_040326_votebymail&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwODZiZDdlZi05MjJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bCAu5oZ7IneFb65gxH-Z3OTtqBVH94uLSTK2Axgzopo=)**

It’s secure and accessible, and it works. Trump knows this — it’s why he voted by mail himself! But he doesn’t care about security or accessibility. He cares about unlawfully influencing elections to stay in power. It’s why he and his allies are determined to undermine trust in voting. = Just like we stopped Trump’s attempts to abuse control of the Oregon National Guard in Portland and cut food assistance for working families, we won’t back down from defending our democracy and the proven system Oregonians trust to make their voices heard. = = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = | = | = | = | | | = | = | | | = | = Thank you! ## Join the Vote No Rally with Governor Youngkin & Congressman McGuire! 🇺🇸

Virginia is at a crossroads. Join us in Lynchburg to help us get out the vote. [RSVP for the rally Here.](https://eky6fg9ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001PfofN056BDv1zpowsApQTBnWZbz_2NZeFclR1ogEWeiQtP4Y-EYS1HOxqtdKs4kGNMwnDif5uvBSAiivqP7MHbzd45vAySgmXS3ym26P2AWqRhmsw9jEezsyNzAQkzg_JIwdf-J5FAeUum9U4bzkM7_bCd9xtBgDeI1OQKXtb-eLMnAr0lLF-jbcag4oukWGZYaqup5SjdNgzSfNR38UJrDd8ez4oilFcoopBXxFHq4Rsy815CAr8Ub54deqKZVgupDHYw_PFbY=&c=wgZOOfA3R1i4GFdBrF5r1aB6s1JzDCRdIwQPn2khcbrC71EOqWn_Dw==&ch=h4U0k-3AocsDnu7h9eT6IIaJEnICqLME8ZG0_HVrl9h335NZk4xuRQ==)

* When: April 11, 2026 | 9:00 AM - 11:00 AM
* Where: Bella Rose, 1220 Graves Mill Rd, Lynchburg, VA
* Special Guest: Governor Glenn Youngkin
* Featured Speakers: Sen. Mark Peake, Sen. Luther Ciphers, and Del. Wendell Walker
* We will give out vote NO To Unfair Maps signs

Get Involved: Learn the latest on redistricting and how you can help through door knocking, phone banking, or outreach. Supported by Turning Point Action and The Family Foundation. Make a plan to knock doors with us after the rally. Get involved, VOTE NO, and please tell a friend.
If you want to join Team McGuire and help us help Trump Save America, please contact Ramona Christian at ramona@mcguireva.com or call her at 434 391 4546.

---
Wishing You A Blessed Good Friday
---
| |
---
| But he was pierced for our transgressions, he was crushed for our iniquities; the punishment that brought us peace was on him. Isaiah 53:5
---
| 2 Bills Passed in 3 Weeks!
---
|
---
We all know results matter and talk is cheap. I'm proud to announce that Team McGuire has successfully passed two critical bills through the House with bipartisan support in just three weeks.
The Wintergreen Emergency Egress Act For 25 years, this issue remained unsolved, with six different past congressional offices in the 5th attempting to find a fix. By the grace of God, my team and I passed this bill out of the House in just six months.
The Make the District of Columbia Safe and Beautiful Act This bill codifies President Trumps Executive Order to keep DC safe and beautiful. Constituents from my district travel to DC everyday for work and leisure. These folks and all who visit deserve a safe capitol.
We are proving with the right work ethic and a commitment to working as a team for success, we can get Washington working again.
More success for you to announce soon. Thank you for allowing me to serve and thank you for your continued support!
---
Appomattox County Sheriff Endorsement
---
|
---
Thank you, Sheriff Richardson, for the endorsement and everything you do to protect your community. I promise to keep working for you and the people of the Fifth District when re-elected. And don't forget to please vote NO to unfair maps by April 21.
---
Louisa County Sheriff Endorsement
---
|
---
| Thank you, Sheriff Lowe, for keeping everyone in Louisa County safe. Thank you, also, for everything you have been doing to help us encourage folks to vote NO to unfair maps on April 21.

Please contact your Senators and ask them to pass the Save America Act.

---
| |

| Please spread the word, even 17 year olds can vote NO right now, if they will be 18 before the November 3rd election.
---
| Vote Ramona Christian Next VA 05 Committee Chair
---
| |
---
| Ramona Christian holds a degree in political science and has been a steadfast leader in the conservative movement. She served on Donald Trump's platform team, was one of just three Fifth District delegates selected to represent President Trump at the Republican National Convention, and she played a key role on the Trump Virginia campaign team, helping organize Trump rallies across the Commonwealth last year. She also serves as Political Director for Congressman John McGuire and she currently serves as the Buckingham County GOP Chair.
Ramona is a no-nonsense leader, a trusted friend, and someone who absolutely will bring people together and get things done. She has my complete and total endorsement for Virginia's 5th Congressional District Committee Chair. To learn more including how to vote for her and to join her team, email Ramona at ramonachristian862@gmail.com

President Trump is back with yet another nonsensical executive order. This one is about creating a nationwide list of verified eligible voters, and restricting mail-in voting.
Putting something in an executive order doesn’t make it true, and it doesn’t make it legal.
That’s why I am — once again — taking him to court in my 64th lawsuit to protect our elections against this lawless bully.
As your next governor, I will never back down from this kind of challenge. I will use every tool at my disposal to protect our state’s Constitutionally-protected right to run our own elections, defend our gold-standard voting laws, and safeguard our right to vote by mail.
**[I’ll be a governor who takes every opportunity to defend our right to vote. So I’m humbly asking: can you make a $5 or $25 contribution to this grassroots campaign today?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128726348/612157897/2078375028?amount=25&refcode=ea.bdbb1be4-942f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&ms=ea.bdbb1be4-942f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZGJiMWJlNC05NDJmLWYxMTEtOWE0OC0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=W8tSIciRP36NBgxwqQbEUwDDn8bjsJ9Dc-2ugQEGe2I=)**

name: Tax Bezos via Our Revolution
subject: Add your name: Bernie vs Bezos
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ stdin# ]Our Revolution Logo

Peter Willis,

Jeff Bezos' Washington Post has come out swinging against Bernie's
proposed wealth tax:

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/36318?source=email-20260403&t=1011&akid=10487%2E5885482%2EkE_Bek ]Bernie Sanders Tweet: Surprise! The Jeff Bezos-owned Washington Post is
against my 5% billionaire wealth tax. I wonder why? If enacted, Bezos
would owe $12 billion in taxes, and an average family of 4 would receive a
$12,000 direct payment. Poor Jeff would be left with just $224 billion to
survive.

Bezos and his billionaire buddies are terrified that they'll finally be
forced to pay up because this bill is enormously popular.

As co-sponsor Rep. Ro Khanna put it: "This is Sen. Sanders' defining
vision for our age. It is the most ambitious and transformative
legislation for our times to tackle inequality in the New Gilded Age." So
the billionaire class is fighting back, and hard, because they're scared.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/36318?source=email-20260403&t=1012&akid=10487%2E5885482%2EkE_Bek ]Let's give the billionaires a reason to be really scared. Add your
name: Tell Congress to support Bernie's Billionaire Wealth Tax NOW!

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/36318?source=email-20260403&t=1013&akid=10487%2E5885482%2EkE_Bek ]ADD YOUR NAME

The bill would be a game changer. 938 people – the very richest Americans
like Elon Musk and Jeff Bezos – would pay a 5% tax on wealth above $1
billion, generating an estimated $4.4 TRILLION over the next decade for
our health care, housing, schools, and more.

If the bill became law, every person in a household earning less than
$150,000 a year would get a one-time $3,000 payment (that's $12,000 for a
family of four!). But it doesn't stop there: The revenue would expand
Medicare and Medicaid to cover more people, build hundreds of thousands of
affordable homes, cap child care costs, and make sure every public school
teacher is paid at least $60,000 a year.

Bernie's bill would FINALLY close the loopholes the rich have written for
themselves. Let's use this moment to show Congress that this is going to
be a litmus test of whether they'll stand with billionaires or stand with
the rest of us.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/36318?source=email-20260403&t=1014&akid=10487%2E5885482%2EkE_Bek ]ADD YOUR NAME

When we organize, we win,

Our Revolution

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/9?t=1001&akid=10487%2E5885482%2EkE_Bek ]Facebook | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/10?t=1002&akid=10487%2E5885482%2EkE_Bek ]Twitter | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/11?t=1003&akid=10487%2E5885482%2EkE_Bek ]Instagram | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/2355?t=1004&akid=10487%2E5885482%2EkE_Bek ]Youtube | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/2356?t=1005&akid=10487%2E5885482%2EkE_Bek ]Website |
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/10247?t=1006&akid=10487%2E5885482%2EkE_Bek ]Sign Up

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/26879?c0=email-20260403.XOuTxm&t=1007&akid=10487%2E5885482%2EkE_Bek ]Our membership swag

Our Revolution is the largest entirely grassroots funded progressive
group in America.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/26880?c0=email-20260403.SRi3uF&t=1008&akid=10487%2E5885482%2EkE_Bek ]Help us defend democracy and take the fight directly to the
establishment by committing to be a Movement Builder with a recurring
donation of just $5 or more a month!

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/26881?c0=email-20260403.Bedbfk&t=1009&akid=10487%2E5885482%2EkE_Bek ]GIVE MONTHLY

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/26882?c0=email-20260403.yJLk45&t=1010&akid=10487%2E5885482%2EkE_Bek ]GIVE ONCE

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:

name: Team Marlene
subject: Spring cleaning out the camera roll!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MARLENE FOR CONGRESS Checks may also be mailed to:
body:
http://secure.actblue.com/donate/mgw_em?refcode=em_header [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/mgw_em?refcode=em_header]

Team,

March was the end of the quarter, but the start of spring in Arizona! Here’s everything we got up to last month:

secure.actblue.com/donate/mgw_em?refcode=260403_tm_recap_img1 [secure.actblue.com/donate/mgw_em?refcode=260403_tm_recap_img1]

Before the month began, we celebrated 22 years of Arizona List’s incredible work getting pro-choice Democratic women elected.

secure.actblue.com/donate/mgw_em?refcode=260403_tm_recap_img2 [secure.actblue.com/donate/mgw_em?refcode=260403_tm_recap_img2]

Marlene marked the one-year mark of her campaign launch, and we turned in the signatures to get Marlene’s name on the ballot.

secure.actblue.com/donate/mgw_em?refcode=260403_tm_recap_img3 [secure.actblue.com/donate/mgw_em?refcode=260403_tm_recap_img3]

In just five days, we squeezed in four meet-and-greets across the district.

secure.actblue.com/donate/mgw_em?refcode=260403_tm_recap_img4 [secure.actblue.com/donate/mgw_em?refcode=260403_tm_recap_img4]

And we still made time for coffee and conversation with voters.

This campaign and the weather will be warming up pretty soon, but we’ll weather the heat together. Thanks for being on Team Marlene!

— Team Marlene

CONTRIBUTE [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/mgw_em?refcode=260403_tm_recap]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP59/1/106088/images/Marlene.png]

About Marlene Galan-Woods
Marlene Galán-Woods is a former broadcast journalist and mother of five who has lived in Arizona for the past 36 years. Her parents fled communist Cuba looking for a better life. Their risk paid off and allowed Marlene to pursue her career as a broadcast journalist which brought her to Arizona. She met the love of her life, Grant Woods, and they raised a family together before he passed away in 2021. Marlene is running for Congress to stand up to extremists and be a voice for all Arizonans. If elected, Marlene will be the first Latina to represent Arizona's 1st district in Congress.

Here’s everything we got up to last month: secure.actblue.com/donate/mgwem?refcode=260403tmrecapimg1 [secure.actblue.com/donate/mgwem?refcode=260403tmrecapimg1] Before the month began, we celebrated 22 years of Arizona List’s incredible work getting pro-choice Democratic women elected. secure.actblue.com/donate/mgwem?refcode=260403tmrecapimg2 [secure.actblue.com/donate/mgwem?refcode=260403tmrecapimg2] Marlene marked the one-year mark of her campaign launch, and we turned in the signatures to get Marlene’s name on the ballot. secure.actblue.com/donate/mgwem?refcode=260403tmrecapimg3 [secure.actblue.com/donate/mgwem?refcode=260403tmrecapimg3] In just five days, we squeezed in four meet-and-greets across the district. secure.actblue.com/donate/mgwem?refcode=260403tmrecapimg4 [secure.actblue.com/donate/mgwem?refcode=260403tmrecapimg4] And we still made time for coffee and conversation with voters. This campaign and the weather will be warming up pretty soon, but we’ll weather the heat together. Thanks for being on Team Marlene! — Team Marlene CONTRIBUTE [] [] About Marlene Galan-Woods Marlene Galán-Woods is a former broadcast journalist and mother of five who has lived in Arizona for the past 36 years. Her parents fled communist Cuba looking for a better life. Their risk paid off and allowed Marlene to pursue her career as a broadcast journalist which brought her to Arizona. She met the love of her life, Grant Woods, and they raised a family together before he passed away in 2021. Marlene is running for Congress to stand up to extremists and be a voice for all Arizonans. If elected, Marlene will be the first Latina to represent Arizona's 1st district in Congress. Hi Friend,

We live in a time where "truth" feels like a moving target.

When I moved from the financial intensity of Wall Street back to the values of the Heartland, my perspective shifted. I started seeing the patterns--the resilience of the American spirit and the disconnect in our modern media.

I wrote this book to bridge that gap.

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4203a47a-ceef-489f-b329-8fdf28114a8f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

Stay grounded,

name: Richard Blumenthal
subject: What happened in the early morning
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blumenthal for Connecticut
body:
11 attempts to finally get it done.

| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128727190/612160541/623074613?refcode=jtk1449-fr-nd1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM3OTQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNGU4ODFmYWYtOTcyZi1mMTExLTlhNDgtMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=dX7cXPMrXKyBbzna0sQTg8h1w0sWb_sjLbDXCYUkzcY=&emci=15eeae71-3a2a-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&emdi=4e881faf-972f-f111-9a48-000d3a14b640&ceid=9329426)
---
Friend, excuse me if I looked tired. It’s because between two and three in the morning last week, the Senate did something we could’ve done weeks ago.
The Senate has done its job to get TSA workers paid. FEMA and the Coast Guard paid. Other essential parts of the DHS paid. Now the House and President Trump should get out of the way. But ICE and the Border Patrol won’t see another dime until we have reform.
Here’s what happened: The Democrats gave Republicans this same deal from the start. We proposed it 11 times on the floor of the United States Senate. Each time, the Republicans were there to block it, to make your airport lines longer, your flights less safe, and for what? So rogue, lawless agents wouldn’t be required to wear bodycams, show their faces, and identify themselves to the very people they are supposed to serve. Those are just some of the reforms we demand and will continue to fight for.
Meanwhile, TSA workers went without paychecks and ICE was deployed. Travelers to and from Connecticut and across the country were forced to wait in hours-long lines. That suffering was completely avoidable, with the only thing standing in the way being exactly what Republicans just agreed to.
As if that weren’t enough, Republicans are continuing to push the SAVE Act, an attempt at blatant voter suppression through collecting voter information to purge voter rolls before November's election. We blocked it. And will continue to block it _._
**Let me be clear, we won the battle, but the fight is not over. [Will you make a donation today to make sure we have the resources for this fight?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128727191/612160543/-1263650200?amounts=5,25,50,100&refcode=jtk1449-fr-nd1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM3OTQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNGU4ODFmYWYtOTcyZi1mMTExLTlhNDgtMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=dX7cXPMrXKyBbzna0sQTg8h1w0sWb_sjLbDXCYUkzcY=)**
| | [Contribute](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/128727192/612160544/-1263650200?amounts=5,25,50,100&refcode=jtk1449-fr-nd1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM3OTQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNGU4ODFmYWYtOTcyZi1mMTExLTlhNDgtMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=dX7cXPMrXKyBbzna0sQTg8h1w0sWb_sjLbDXCYUkzcY=)
---
Thank you,

Dick

body:
This is a longer email, but I respectfully ask you to read it start to finish:
[](https://links.trueamericanconservatives.com/s/c/UK4f0HwzJQvFRdroorVUwyl3fzT2HZHBUI4hGNN3eElViq6HeXVPQPONfBFo7yDX_QsQqwqQpkE5Brg_iUK9CMnGhpkys_A_8OlmXPXymCDMl1r9TZaQHiVALy5EnEUDxJ2ggFwhKsRHjuBJdlwyqECx_h84n9_X1JXII-umG6af_eKa4w28UxJI1ft6Hp-czEUaz2Y1SDd6DgyMNcQ6Wui0rlSOyexViZLsOCN80SShWSNO02xJKqZm774sU2soKphOwqWJviKHIfSqYaiSHlNjqishwhzLY5wwR1p_GYNmzO496-i73vTECbZlR_NVVHEQC7uE8RVMDoRbfEPxCsbx7iFwFnVn5jPeMSfFp0aGc83PH3Dmj70FDC8JMQNcZFappAjEv056MEHdJ1JIiSc9d_-KELzmbd0q6KojG_u0iyFBnoBW7RiE8YnPBojRKD4Xriht5ePzJ6bZqdIqXfAhkCAACn22vXbeNlKbiqnuXeaNlnFphGyfsWoVNYqlylSoNOHJJydu76TspFAABjGDslfGRgy7SFPqH-QGlrlv12LiHe-2c0NlspOYb9S4Cx4QX5KHjbmml8goR-05mNPEQX9we6_vgxcXv9bb7WlzwSEc3Bk9hWS5SApQjU--u_Fik2DRAgo7zisa9K2UWIiuBZjm9LDo5kiNdYf57AbH2XPyiRolYWOde-Gz_bHBd-ssBUC6ZmpJOEOahI2z3-Y7ers58I32L_0a9edevto02wIYFhHWAfLJY_FsaIgFpuwe/wNSI8APIDkuPZ92NQM88PbEzGH1g2Sig/19)My fellow Americans - it is Vice President JD Vance.
[This is a longer email, but I respectfully ask you to read it start to finish.](https://links.trueamericanconservatives.com/s/c/IG1U82weeNrTgiquuvVQYnVIrosGe9vYMIqA4StrA59H26fDMUwczvY7SR-BTCjncEl7wcmxe2znuq4fMT2SkGh5fskpe8Pb7xD2ULCmkPygBF7JS6mwL0dBRCR3Sj2elQwm0z8NWh1INwMtmsoQ_fTr1i_-YzR2OQlMHgr43OH6u509kYjWDpkZi5y9J_itNyXJV6xtnZ6e2pRZObat32bGfWQObmEGanC4ppVmL6bks0uElmSThP1f_mXOE-3oGr5pmxrpV_wLMLJkbSxR2qx01MxS83eIfYekTKdekYkpouyaImHegixCxdPZ8s9DvQfzjx1h-5_GovZVBHvIfsbNj-cV8xBAVB3rP98Ywlw-tZ0xiR-62DkO7pPJ6wDpwVjYAH_dxt60yw4V5DcQ9KiPsVwOeLDyD9i21yRKlBhxyuRD5hEYPFnfozz4LbggC_Pp5_c_gKc5dxn2cOv1UIlgwA5DK2w52UnxtC2BO1RM9AFcAcMv09CR8ZK62aYq_tpe_4hUHFhFhVGjjLESRxXQIMGH2iES_dLoiZEhvGyt4uq31X1dsci85KA-Ww0zwvW5qf2_Jes9DYdOkebqyRTwf5XKNOJNBwHRVY7qvfRjqx34sS3YBoUg7c4gD-s5Ou1W9UB88S_l8q0dWLSA-7HS2MOzLhecd9PbZW4KiADrLLw1msLAWgbPMnGsn6pHHxdwoGNnnYSUy_v2YhqgkRmAU_dsGidL1NrM1YsaopDgGjjHRQ3LUYHFGyYDFLuJLItK/dbL48UGYK3ueCVCaJltr4MFi9pCNtLwI/19)
It wasn't easy telling the world I grew up dirt poor, I was raised by my Mamaw while my mother struggled with addiction.
It wasn't exactly the prep school/trust fund/summer-in-the-Hamptons upbringing that is commonplace in DC.
But I felt God called me to tell my story. Warts and all. I felt led to speak out because I believe millions of Americans share stories like mine. Stories of jobs being outsourced to China, addiction ravaging communities and killing our neighbors, and heeding the call of duty after the Towers fell on 9/11.
As your Vice President, I now stand in the breach for the left-behind, forgotten men and women who built this country, but were abandoned by the political class in DC.
I'll do everything in my power to make sure that never happens again. I'll fight every day for good folks like you.
I promise to always stand between you and the Democrat Machine that aims to obliterate everything that makes our country great.

clean_body: This is a longer email, but I respectfully ask you to read it start to finish: fellow Americans - it is Vice President JD Vance. It wasn't easy telling the world I grew up dirt poor, I was raised by my Mamaw while my mother struggled with addiction. It wasn't exactly the prep school/trust fund/summer-in-the-Hamptons upbringing that is commonplace in DC. But I felt God called me to tell my story. Warts and all. I felt led to speak out because I believe millions of Americans share stories like mine. Stories of jobs being outsourced to China, addiction ravaging communities and killing our neighbors, and heeding the call of duty after the Towers fell on 9/11. As your Vice President, I now stand in the breach for the left-behind, forgotten men and women who built this country, but were abandoned by the political class in DC. I'll do everything in my power to make sure that never happens again. I'll fight every day for good folks like you. I promise to always stand between you and the Democrat Machine that aims to obliterate everything that makes our country great. We're coming out of the most expensive election in American history. I mean that. Kamala Harris spent over a billion dollars against us. It took EVERY PENNY we had to get our story out there, expose her ultra-Liberal record, reach undecided voters, and win. So now we're forced to rebuild. The Democrat Machine is ALREADY crushing us in fundraising, ALREADY outspending us in key races, and ALREADY building the campaign infrastructure to decimate us in the Midterm Elections. At this point, the consequences of simply resting on our laurels would be unthinkable. So here's my final pitch to you: I understand that's ambitious. But if that's what it takes to deliver President Trump a Republican House for his entire second term, it's a no-brainer. I'll do what it takes to win. Do you think you can help us get there? Whether it's $5 or $500 - the amount you give isn't nearly as important as how many folks step up: , you've probably heard bits and pieces of my story at this point. If my humble beginnings taught me anything, it's to never give up, never surrender, and never go down without a fight. That's why I'm partnering with Steve Scalise to help lay the critical groundwork we need to win. Regardless, it's an honor to fight alongside you. God bless America, Vice President JD Vance \- Democrat Leadership formed an anti-Trump task force \- More than a dozen Attorneys General are suing the Trump Administration \- A House Democrat just introduced 7 Articles of Impeachment If they manage to strip us of our House Majority, they 'll have the power to impeach, investigate, and bring his entire legislative agenda to a SCREECHING HALT! subject: Following up from yesterday

body:
It's a small act that makes a huge difference
| | | | |
---
Hey, Peter, we’re following up from yesterday.
President Trump and the entire GOP establishment are firing on all cylinders to crush our grassroots movement, and the only way we can fight back and flip this seat blue is with an airtight campaign strategy shaped by people like you.
**[Please, will you take our priorities survey right away? Roy can’t win this toss-up race without people like you in his corner.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/8479?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&t=6&akid=939%2E124072%2EPyc172)**
Letting us know what issues matter to you is a small act that will make a huge difference. Thank you.

Team Cooper
_\---------- Forwarded message ----------
_ From: **Roy Cooper**
Date: Thur, Apr 2, 2026
Subject: I need to hear from you directly
| |
| | |
---
[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/8482?t=7&akid=939%2E124072%2EPyc172)
Peter,

The first quarterly FEC deadline of the year is now behind us, and thanks to this grassroots team, our momentum is going strong. I am so grateful.
While my MAGA opponent is backed by far-right super PACs and the GOP establishment, I’m building a grassroots movement powered by folks like you, and your interests, concerns and hopes for the future.
**That’s why I need to hear from you directly. This race is picking up steam, and your input is critical to making sure we flip North Carolina and the Senate blue.[Will you take my priorities survey and let me know what issues matter most to you in this election?](https://act.roycooper.com/go/8479?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&t=8&akid=939%2E124072%2EPyc172)**
| | [TAKE THE SURVEY](https://act.roycooper.com/go/8479?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&t=9&akid=939%2E124072%2EPyc172)
---
With experts at Politico saying that Democrats have no chance to take back the Senate without winning in North Carolina, this is the most important race in the country.
I’m the biggest threat to the GOP’s hold on power, so President Trump and Republicans are going all-in to defeat me. **Now, a recent poll shows this race statistically tied.**
The only way I can fight back and flip this seat blue is with an airtight campaign strategy shaped by people like you. **[Before the end of the day, will you take just one minute to sh are your thoughts with me?](https://act.roycooper.com/go/8479?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&t=10&akid=939%2E124072%2EPyc172)**
| | [TAKE THE SURVEY](https://act.roycooper.com/go/8479?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&t=11&akid=939%2E124072%2EPyc172)
---
Thank you for adding your voice.

Roy Cooper
| |
* * *
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.
